title: 熱炒店聊天
body: 23: Dinner's on me. Lawrence, you order. Lawrence Chang: Me? Anything I want? 23: Yeah. Let's see what you can come up with.;<> 23: Jason, anything you'd like to avoid? Allergy or something?;<>;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Nope. I'm OK with anything. Lawrence Chang: OK. ->No raw stuff, please. Lawrence Chang: Roger! <<#EndSimple>> 23: Go ahead, Lawrence.;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: Sure!;<> //隆生去點菜 <> <<#Wait(3)>> 23: ...;<> 23: How are you feeling? Your first day as a spy.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->It's cool. 23: Haha, I can't tell if you mean it or not. <<#Simple(8)>> ->I mean it. 23: Good for you! ->Not really. 23: Haha! Yeah, I can relate. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->It's complicated. 23: Haha! 23: Sometimes you're just like Nin. ->I thought real spy work should be more badass? 23: What you did was close enough, haha! ->... <<#EndSimple>> Thought: She's more like her usual self now. Thought: In the safehouse she was acting kinda weird...;<> //隆生走回來坐上椅子(應該是副流程) <> <<#Wait(2)>> Lawrence Chang: Hey, what are you guys talking about? Lawrence Chang: Relationship gossip? 23: We're not hormones-driven teenagers, OK? Lawrence Chang: Hey! Time for some fun conversations after a long day!;<> Lawrence Chang: How's it going? Nin agreed to go out with you yet?;<> Thought: Huh? 23's in love with Situ...?;<> 23: Of course!;<> 23: Who could say no when I come asking, eh? Lawrence Chang: Wait. Did she agree to just go out with you, or go out with you?;<> 23: ... 23: ...I think she's going out with me.;<> Thought: ...Does that mean a yes or no? Lawrence Chang: But that's like no progress at all!;<> 23: I know right? She just doesn't get it!;<> 23: Worst of all, she's complaining to me all the time this job is all work no date.;<> Thought: I can't imagine Situ saying something like that. 23: I mean, come on! Here's your date sitting in front of you!;<> Lawrence Chang: Hahahahaha! Lawrence Chang: You might need a few tips from Jason.;<>;<> 23: Yeah, the only one who's not single...;<> Lawrence Chang: Teach 23 a move or two. What would you do when your crush is oblivious to your feelings? 23: Can he give me any lesson, though? 23: He looks like the oblivious kind himself.;<> Thought: ... <<#Simple(8)>> ->No, I'm not! Lawrence Chang: Yeah, that is rude, 23! ->Yeah, I am. 23: See? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: Whatever. What would you do, Jason? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Why don't you just tell her straight out? <<@我怕破壞現在的關係，到時候很尷尬欸。>> 23: Nope.;<>;<> 23: It's gonna be super awkward if she says no. I can't risk what I have now. Lawrence Chang: You're thinking waaaay too far ahead.;<> 23: I'm a sensitive girl in love!;<> Lawrence Chang: My ass. ->Just try harder? 23: In other words, telling her straight out? <<#GoTo(@我怕破壞現在的關係，到時候很尷尬欸。)>> ->Maybe she's not oblivious. She's just not into you. 23: Damn! 23: You don't know how to be diplomatic, do you? Haha!;<> Lawrence Chang: Can't believe you're able to laugh it off...;<> ->I don't have anything to contribute. Lawrence Chang: Huh. 23: Yeah. I'm knee-deep in it and I've got nothing to contribute, either.;<>;<> <<#EndSimple>> 23: Hey! I don't have to be with her as a couple! 23: A colleague relationship is totally fine by me!;<> Lawrence Chang: Not that quitter talk again.;<> 23: Shut up.;<> 23: I mean, for real, being able to work with her as a team is good enough for me.;<> Lawrence Chang: Nin doesn't care about you enough, if you ask me.;<> 23: That's because you've never seen her off-work face. 23: She's really nice to me.;<> Lawrence Chang: The hell? Am I supposed to be jealous? <<#Simple(8)>> ->I think so, yeah. 23: Oi! Quiet! ->Jealous of what? Not being able to get a date? 23: Aaaargh!!!;<>;//黑人嗆爆圖 Lawrence Chang: Oooooo!!;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: That hurts. 23: No shit.;<>;<>;<> Thought: Was I too blunt? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: Say, I never suspected this operation is behind OWL's back. 23: Dude... 23: That's what you'd call an awkward change of subject. Lawrence Chang: This feels like a good time to talk about it. 23: ...;<> 23: Sorry for not telling you. 23: For the record, it's all Nin's idea. I was dead set against it.;<> Lawrence Chang: Yeah, just dump it all on Nin. She's not here anyway.;<> Thought: 23 blew a raspberry. Lawrence Chang: Ah, that explains the small van.;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: OWL didn't give you the budget for it.;<> 23: No, that has nothing to do with the budget. A small van works better for the mission.;<>;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Aren't you concerned that OWL might find out? 23: We are. That's why we're performing our regular duties, as well.;<> 23: If we get caught, the worst they could do is to call this pet project off. Lawrence Chang: Will Nin give up that easily?;<>;<> 23: ...I don't think so.;<> Lawrence Chang: Will you stand by her, if/when that happens?;<> 23: ...;<> 23: Probably.;<> Lawrence Chang: Awwww, true love. 23: Hey.;<> ->Does OWL have that much money to spare? 23: That much and then some, if the big bosses feel like spending.;<> 23: We are backed by all sorts of big corps, you know! <> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: Illegal investigation is not something you would do, 23. 23: It's private investigation, not illegal.;<> Lawrence Chang: I think we broke a ton of laws in the process? 23: ...You're not wrong.;<> Lawrence Chang: Aren't you always going like...;<> Lawrence Chang: We are the law enforcement!;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: Breaking the law to enforce the law is WRONG!;<> Lawrence Chang: And blah blah?;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Right. Why did you agree to do this? // ->And blah blah? 23: When did I ever blah?;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: You get the idea! Lawrence Chang: Don't try to dodge it, 23!;<>;<> 23: Whatever. ->... <<#EndSimple>> 23: ...;<> 23: I'm doing this for Nin's sake.;<> 23: I can't let her start a mission without support, can I? Lawrence Chang: ... 23: Put that starry-eyed look away, Lawrence! I'm warning you.;<>;<> 23: ... 23: Now, seriously, I don't think what we're doing is right.;<>;<> 23: I've been working for OWL long enough to see a fair share of moral dilemma...;<> 23: By now I have developed my own rules and red lines.;<> Thought: Nin was right about 23... 23: My bottom line is that law and order must be respected.;<> Thought: I like that idea. <> <<#Simple(8)>> ->I like that idea.;<>;//有選這個藥紀錄 23: Then you're on the conservative side, too.;<> 23: However... <> ->Respect? Or obey? 23: Obedience without independent thinking is bad. ->... <<#EndSimple>> 23: Law and order are not perfect. There are real life cases where they don't apply very well.;<> 23: In a perfect world, I'd totally follow them without questioning, but this world is not perfect.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Respect, obey, they're close enough. 23: "Close enough" is a vague definition. I can't argue against something that is not properly defined. ->What do you mean by "respect", then? // ->... <<#EndSimple>> 23: I mean we must uphold the authority. 23: Laws exist to create order.;<>;<> 23: We all know laws aren't perfect, but in the overwhelming majority of cases, we must follow them.;<> 23: Try not to break any law, even if we are doing the right thing.;<>;<> 23: Or if we have to break it from time to time, we should feel ashamed of it. 23: We must face the fact when we take inappropriate actions.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->That is blind obedience. //if剛剛說喜歡想法 <<#If($DLStateFloat=1)>> 23: I thought you'd agree, when you said you like the idea. <<#EndIf>> 23: I'm not doubting the law itself, or trying to suggest nothing could go wrong as long as you obey it. 23: What I'm trying to say is, the moral cost of breaking the law is often underrated.;<> Thought: ...Is she referring to Situ?;<> 23: I put an emphasis on the word "respect", didn't I?;<> ->Agreed. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: Oh... <<#Simple(8)>> ->What's up? ->You agree with her? Lawrence Chang: No... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: You're starting to lose me. This is getting philosophical quickly...;<>;<> 23: Sometimes I want to be you. Lawrence Chang: Oi! I can tell it's not a compliment!;<>;<> 23: Good for you.;<> Lawrence Chang: Anyway, as far as I can tell, you're saying breaking the law is wrong. 23: Right, if you have to simplify things...;<> Lawrence Chang: Then why are you doing this knowing it's wrong?;<> 23: We don't always get to do what we believe is right. We choose what we want. 23: I want to be with Nin. Plain and simple.;<>;<> 23: ...;<> Thought: Not the right thing, but the desired thing? Lawrence Chang: That brings us back to the starting point! 23: You're right, haha... Thought: Her eyes are not laughing... 23: ...;<>;//23開始喝酒，接下來時不時一直喝酒 Lawrence Chang: I say you and Nin are focusing too much on fine details! Lawrence Chang: Just be yourself and be proud of it! Right, Jason?;<>;<>;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Right. Lawrence Chang: See?;<> ->Sometimes I'm not sure what "be myself" means. Lawrence Chang: R-really...? 23: Well said, Jason.;<> ->You're over-simplifying things. Lawrence Chang: Huh? Am I? <> <<#EndSimple>> 23: ... 23: Say, Lawrence.;<> Lawrence Chang: What's with that tone? I sense a hearty talk coming?;<> 23: You seriously want to join OWL? Lawrence Chang: Of course! 23: ...;<> 23: I don't know if it's the right place for a simple guy like you...;<> Lawrence Chang: Is there anything wrong with being simple? 23: Well, I don't know about that...;<> 23: Pretty sure you will pass the adversity test in one shot, though.;<> Lawrence Chang: Ha! Even you have to recognize my talent!;<> Thought: 23's grin doesn't look like recognition? <> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Adversity test? What's that? 23: See? You have lots to learn, Lawrence. Lawrence Chang: Huh?;<> 23: Lesson One. If you don't know something, ask.;<>;<> 23: Don't make a fool of yourself. Lawrence Chang: Eh? Passing the adversity test? Is that a bad thing?;<>;<>;<> ->What about me?;<> 23: What, you want to join OWL, too?;<>;<> Thought: Hasn't Nin told her about me? Lawrence Chang: Oh yeah! We are comrades, Jason! 23: The adversity test?;<> 23: It won't be too much a challenge for you, I guess. <> ->... <<#EndSimple>> 23: The adversity test is something designed by Grandma to find out how reliable an agent is. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Reliable? 23: Whether or not they will perform as expected on missions. Lawrence Chang: Then passing the test is obviously a good thing! 23: A simple guy you are. ->Some sort of evaluation? 23: Yes, but not the kind you have in mind. ->... <<#EndSimple>> 23: Passing the test means Grandma believes she has complete domination over you. Lawrence Chang: Wut? 23: In her logic, the adversity test is not intended to find out how loyal we are or tame us.;<> 23: The purpose is to find our nature. 23: Loyalty and obedience degrade over time. The world changes. People change.;<> 23: What remains constant, according to Grandma, is a person's nature. If you get that, you have full control.;<> Lawrence Chang: What's that supposed to mean? 23: One example, the trolley problem. A runaway trolley is barreling down the tracks... 23: On one track is Frances, on the other are five kids. You control the switch. Which side would you save?;<> Thought: Fran.;<> <> <<@問火車難題囉壓>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Fran. 23: That was quick. You're one cold-blooded bastard.;<> 23: Now Grandma knows when you should be put in a cell, instead of on the front lines.;<> 23: Oh, and she has a trump card to play when you show the slightest sign of going renegade.;<> ->The kids. 23: Really? I thought you'd say Frances. 23: Now Grandma knows you value the greater good over Frances.;<> ->Can't I just holler really loud and tell them to get the hell out? ($DLStateFloat);//選過消失 <> 23: It's a moral question! Don't dwell on fine details. 23: One Frances, or five kids. Which do you choose? <<#GoTo(@問火車難題囉壓)>> <<#EndSimple>> 23: Of course, that was just a hypothetical scenario. 23: Grandma puts you in real life scenarios with real consequences.;<> 23: It takes most agents three or four tries to "pass" it.;<> 23: Nin has failed it 21 times and still counting.;<> Lawrence Chang: What? How?;<> 23: She managed to stop the trolley.;<> Lawrence Chang: Huh?;<> 23: Instead of making a choice, she finds a way to circumvent the whole situation. 23: Unlike the hypothetical scenario, in real life there's always a perfect solution without sacrificing anyone.;<> 23: A lot of the times we can see this perfect solution, but couldn't pull it off. 23: Nin does it every single time, leaping out of Grandma's trap.;<> Lawrence Chang: That is...COOL. 23: Yeah, totally.;<> 23: However, that means she has yet to pass the test. Lawrence Chang: There will be more? 23: You bet, and it gets tougher every single time...;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Will you be informed of upcoming adversity tests? 23: Of course not. ->What if someone fails the test? 23: Then there's the retest, re-retest, so on and so forth. Matter of time.;<> 23: Each time you come out with more scars. Eventually Grandma will break you. <> ->... <<#EndSimple>> 23: These tests are mixed in our daily missions. You never know until it's right in your face. Lawrence Chang: Does she have to go that far?;<> 23: Grandma believes so. She must have complete domination over all agents.;<> 23: Don't you know the dynamics within OWL? Lawrence Chang: Oh, I do know that...;<> Thought: Dynamics? <<#Simple(8)>> ->What do you mean by dynamics? // ->Are there, like, opposing factions? 23: Oh? You're sharp, aren't you?;<> 23: That was spot-on. <> ->... <<#EndSimple>> 23: Let me explain. 23: OWL is a government law enforcement agency in theory. Under the table, it's backed by big corporations.;<> 23: Multiple corporations have considerable influence over OWL.;<> 23: Most obviously, agent drafting.;<> 23: There are three ways to become an OWL agent: recommendation, recruitment, and special cases.;<> 23: Every agent with outstanding performance are allowed to recommend candidates that they see fit. Lawrence Chang: Nin is able to do that! 23: Correct. Recommended candidates must be reviewed by Grandma, though.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Can you make recommendations? 23: Nope.;<> Lawrence Chang: HA!;<> 23: Damn you. You've got to be superhuman to earn recommendation rights.;<> 23: The logic is that talents recognized by these inhuman beings can't be much worse.;<>;<> 23: You should see Nin's mentor. One tough piece of work.;<> 23: But very few could join OWL by this way.;<> ->I guess Lawrence is counting on her recommendation? 23: Right!;<> 23: He must earn Nin's trust first.;<> 23: Anyway, very few could join OWL by this way. <> ->... <<#EndSimple>> 23: As to recruitment, submit your application and go through the routine. 23: The reality is, you must be backed by corporations to make the cut.;<>;<> 23: About 90% of OWL agents are recruited this way. Thought: That means OWL is full of corporate representatives.;<> 23: Remember when I said this operation would be called off if OWL caught us?;<>;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Because you're jeopardizing regular missions? 23: On the surface, yes. 23: The real reason is, we're crossing a red line. ->Because Vision is one of OWL's backers? 23: Exactly. ->... <<#EndSimple>> 23: Vision will try everything it has to stop us.;<> 23: And being one of the Big Three, its influence is massive.;<> Thought: Situ's not bullshitting when she said this investigation has immense risk...;<> Lawrence Chang: Hold on. Why did you say I'd pass the adversity test in one go?;<> 23: Because you're that simple to figure out!;<> 23: Hardly need any test, if I were Grandma! Lawrence Chang: Oi! //if剛剛有問自己好不好通過 <<#If($DLStateFloat2=1)>> Thought: She said it shouldn't be too much of a challenge to me. Thought: Does that mean I'm simple-minded, too? <<#EndIf>> 23: *Hic* Are you taking offense or what?;<> Lawrence Chang: No. 23: Or do you see yourself as a mysterious guy?;<> Lawrence Chang: No, I didn't!;<>;<> 23: Bullshit. 23: You scratch your head every time you lie.;<> Lawrence Chang: Ah!;<> 23: Told you to practice lying. *Hic* 23: Now, hear this, Jason.;<> 23: There are telling signs when people lie.;<> 23: Shifty eyes, fidgeting fingers, gotta keep those under control. 23: Or sometimes fake it to mislead.;<> Lawrence Chang: Is it appropriate to teach him that?;<> 23: Nah, not a problem at all!;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: You're drunk. 23: No, I'm not!;<> Lawrence Chang: See, Jason? One problem of 23's is that she can't hold her liquor...;<>;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Is she doing OK now? ->Color me surprised. ->... <<#EndSimple>> 23: Huh? He can't hold his liquor? 23: Come on, dude! Be a man!;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: I was talking about you! Lawrence Chang: She's totally lost it... 23: Heard you! I'm sober! Lawrence Chang: Then answer me. Of all people in this world, who do you like best? 23: Nin!;<> Lawrence Chang: Why? 23: Because she's awesome, and she's nice to me. Lawrence Chang: Yeah, she's lost it.;<>;<>;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Shall we walk her back? Lawrence Chang: Not yet! This is an opportunity. Thought: What's he planning? ->Does she often get drunk? Lawrence Chang: No. She seldom drinks on duty. Lawrence Chang: Maybe she's under a lot of stress these days. ->... <<#EndSimple>> 23: Nin's like, super nice, to me...;<> Lawrence Chang: I know. You said it already.;<> Lawrence Chang: Now, let's try something else.;<> Lawrence Chang: Are you really OK with just being a colleague of hers? 23: ...Huh?;<>;//突然坐正 23: Told you yes.;<>;//在這裡方摸衣領，盡可能持續久一點 Thought: She's suddenly sobering up. Lawrence Chang: Damn. Thought I'd catch you this time. 23: Catch what? I have nothing to hide! *Urk* 23: Okay. Gotta go before I barf all over the place.;<> 23: Bye. Lawrence Chang: Bye.;<>;//23淡出離開 <<#Simple(8)>> ->Bye. // ->I'll walk you back. 23: A gentleman, huh. Learn your lesson, Lawrence! Lawrence Chang: Yeah, yeah, I know. Go get some rest. ->... <<#EndSimple>> //23淡入淡出走回家 <<#End>>

body:

title: 回司徒基地
body:
//大家歸位:主角跟張隆生坐在沙發上，23在座位上，大家表情都有點。
Lawrence Chang: Phew! Home, sweet home.;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_便服_基地正坐左看)>>;<<Play(程芳,MBU_程芳_便服_正坐右看)>>
23: This is not your home.;<<Set($DLState,23講話鏡頭轉四人定位)>>;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_便服3_基地正坐左看)>>
Lawrence Chang: Home is where your family is.;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_便服_基地正坐左看返回)>>
23: ...
23: ...And we're not your family.;<<Play(程芳,MBU_程芳_便服_正坐右看轉左)>>;<<Play(23,MBU_二十三_基地側坐看內)>>
Lawrence Chang: Heeey! Why so serious?;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_便服3_基地正坐左看轉右)>>
Lawrence Chang: You're— OUCH!;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_便服_基地正坐摀胸)>>;<<Play(程芳,MBU_程芳_便服_正坐左看轉右)>>
23: Stay still. Don't forget your injury.
Lawrence Chang: Hmph...;<<Play(程芳,MBU_程芳_便服_正坐右看摀嘴)>>
Lawrence Chang: Hey, Jason, you didn't even have a scratch!;<<PlayUntil(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_便服_基地正坐摀胸左看)>>;<<Play(23,MBU_二十三_基地側坐看內返回)>>
Lawrence Chang: OUCH! OUCH!;<<PlayUntil(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_便服_基地正坐摀胸左看返回)>>;<<Play(程芳,MBU_程芳_便服_正坐右看摀嘴返回)>>
<<#Simple(8)>>
->Are you OK?
    Lawrence Chang: Not too bad...
->Quiet!
    Lawrence Chang: That was mean...;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_便服_基地正坐摀胸左看)>>
    Lawrence Chang: I'm hurting, for real...
    <<PlayUntil(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_便服_基地正坐摀胸左看返回)>>
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Lawrence Chang: I see 23 has been skipping her first aid lessons!
23: Sorry about that...;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_便服3_基地正坐右看轉左)>>;<<Play(23,MBU_二十三_基地側坐低頭)>>
Lawrence Chang: Eh? I was trying to be funny. Don't take it seriously...;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_便服_基地正坐摀胸返回)>>
Thought: Hm? I think Fran is signaling me something...
Lawrence Chang: What's Nin doing?;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_便服3_基地正坐左看轉右)>>
Lawrence Chang: She's been hiding in the bedroom since we came back.;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_便服_基地正坐左看)>>
23: ...;<<Play(程芳,MBU_程芳_便服_正坐右看轉左)>>;<<Play(23,MBU_二十三_基地側坐低頭返回)>>
23: ...;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_便服3_基地正坐右看轉左)>>;<<Play(23,MBU_二十三_基地側坐看內)>>
Lawrence Chang: Hey, 23!!!;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_便服_基地正坐左看返回)>>
23: Quiet. She's on the phone.
Lawrence Chang: Ah, yeah...
<<PlayUntil(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_便服_基地正坐左看)>>
<<#End>>

title: 出大樓遇到隆生他們在轉扭蛋
body:

Lawrence Chang: ...Nope.
23: Again, huh...;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_便服_背站驚訝返回)>>
<<#Simple(99999)>>
->Playing with gacha machines?
->Aren't we going to dinner?
<<#EndSimple>>
Lawrence Chang: Hey Jason! I don't know what's wrong with 23 today. She said I can play as much as I want. She's paying!;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_便服_背站右看)>>
23: Watch your tongue.
23: Gotta throw him a bone or two every once in a while.;<<Play(23,MBU_二十三_背身抬手)>>
Lawrence Chang: That makes me sound like a puppy.;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_便服_背站右看轉左)>>
23: Be a good boy and play your game.;<<Play(23,MBU_二十三_背身抬手返回)>>
Lawrence Chang: Yeah! I'll definitely get THE ONE!;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_便服_背站左看返回)>>

Lawrence Chang: Ugh! Not again!
Lawrence Chang: OK! I give up!;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_便服_背站激動返回)>>
Lawrence Chang: Dinner time. Let's go.;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_便服_背站右看)>>
23: Open air diner?;<<Play(23,MBU_二十三_背身抬頭)>>
Lawrence Chang: Same old place?;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_便服_背站右看轉左)>>
23: Problem?
Lawrence Chang: No. I'll order beer this time!
23: Sure thing!;<<Play(23,MBU_二十三_背身抬頭返回)>>
23: Come, Jason!
<<#End>>

title: 手搖杯
body:
Kent Liu: Welcome to Popo! What would you like today?;<<Play(劉冠廷,MBU_店面人物_店員A_左看)>>
Thought: This is not my cup of tea. I'll just order one for Lawrence...
<<Set($OrderDrinkWrong,1)>>
<<#Simple(99999)>>
->One nata jelly bubble tea.
    //
->One bubble bubble tea.
->One nata jelly black tea.
->One pearl black tea.
    <<Set($OrderDrinkWrong,-1)>>
<<#EndSimple>>
Kent Liu: Ice and sugar?;<<Play(劉冠廷,MBU_店面人物_店員A_左看返回)>>
<<#Simple(99999)>>
->Less ice, no sugar.
    <<Set($OrderDrinkWrong,-1)>>
->No ice, half sugar.
    //
->Less ice, half sugar.
->No ice, no sugar.
    <<Set($OrderDrinkWrong,-1)>>
<<#EndSimple>>
Kent Liu: Any topping?
<<#Simple(99999)>>
->Extra herbal jelly.
    <<Set($OrderDrinkWrong,-1)>>
->Extra bubbles.
    //
->Extra nata jelly.
->Extra crystal jelly.
    <<Set($OrderDrinkWrong,-1)>>
<<#EndSimple>>
Kent Liu: The total is $180.;<<Play(劉冠廷,MBU_店面人物_店員A_左看)>>
Thought: Bloody robbery.
Kent Liu: Working on it.;<<Play(劉冠廷,MBU_店面人物_店員A_左看返回)>>
Thought: A cup of bubble tea used to cost like $90. Gone are the good old days.
Thought: I should have got the order correct... Right?

title: 睡前與程芳談心
body: //程芳躺在床上 Frances Cheng: Welcome back!;<>;<> <> <<#Simple(8)>> ->How are you feeling? Frances Cheng: Not bad. ->I thought you'd be asleep? Frances Cheng: I'm waiting for you. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Thought: ...Something's bugging her. <<#Simple(10)>> ->Something's on your mind? Frances Cheng: With so many things going on, I have a ton on my mind, haha... ->It's going to be over soon. Frances Cheng: Hopefully. ->I need some rest. Frances Cheng: Feeling tired?; Thought: You have no idea. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->You have no idea. Frances Cheng: Yeah...;<> Frances Cheng: Let's grab some sleep. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Or do you want to talk? Frances Cheng: I do!;<> <<#GoTo(@和芳聊聊第三日的事情)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: Good night.;//程芳翻身睡覺;<> Thought: I need a shower. <<#End>> //玩家互動進浴室後話面黑掉 ->Or do you want to talk? Frances Cheng: I do!;<> <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<@和芳聊聊第三日的事情>> Frances Cheng: I've given it more thought, what we talked about before you went to the farm. Thought: Fran doesn't want me to participate in their mission...;<> Frances Cheng: Then you were almost killed there!;<> Thought: But I can't pull out now.;<> Frances Cheng: If we still can't find any evidence tomorrow, the situation will only get worse.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->You don't know that. Frances Cheng: I do know. ->Today is just bad luck. Frances Cheng: There will be more bad luck tomorrow. ->I agree. Frances Cheng: Right? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: The way I see it, Ms. Situ brought up your condition to remind you of two things.;<> Frances Cheng: First, OWL will be after you. Second, she will send you on more dangerous missions. <<#Simple(8)>> ->You're reading too much into it, Fran. Frances Cheng: I don't think so. ->That makes sense. // ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: J, I know we have gone too far to pull out.;<> Frances Cheng: I want you to watch out for yourself, because I don't think they will try very hard to protect you. <<#Simple(8)>> ->You don't trust them? Frances Cheng: Correct. ->I can look after myself. Frances Cheng: I know. Still, promise me you will take care. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->OK. Frances Cheng: Good. ->I'm already super cautious. Frances Cheng: Ha, I knew you'd say that. Thought: She knows she can't convince me. <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: I'm sure that Ms. Situ and the rest of them are good people.;<> Frances Cheng: But they may not be as reliable as they seem. Thought: I won't trust Lawrence too much, but Situ...?;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->You think they're not capable? // ->They look pretty OK to me, except for Lawrence, that is. Frances Cheng: Haha, Lawrence will feel hurt if he heard that. Frances Cheng: ... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: I'm talking about their heart. <<#Simple(10)>> ->Huh? Are we suddenly getting poetic? Frances Cheng: No! ->They all have brave hearts. Frances Cheng: I do not doubt that. ->I think I know what you're getting at. Frances Cheng: Yeah. Thought: She doesn't believe it... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: Ms. Situ is an excellent special agent, but she lacks something that makes us human. <> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Weak spots.;<> ->Emotions?;<> Frances Cheng: More or less.;<> ->Complaisance? ->Sense of humor? Frances Cheng: No... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: She is detached, approaching everything from miles away, as if it's someone else's problem.;<> <<#Simple(10)>> ->I thought that is a good thing. Frances Cheng: I don't think so. ->She may hurt people around her without being aware of it. Frances Cheng: Yes.;<> Thought: She seems surprised to hear me say that... Frances Cheng: You've grown more perceptive, J.;<> ->Maybe her detachment is just on the surface. Frances Cheng: I think it comes from the inside. ->... <<#EndSimple>> //if選軟弱 <<#If($DLStateFloat=1)>> Frances Cheng: As you said, she doesn't seem to have any weak spot at all. <<#EndIf>> //if選情緒 <<#If($DLStateFloat=2)>> Frances Cheng: As you said, she seems to have her emotions perfectly under control. <<#EndIf>> Frances Cheng: But that is inhuman... Frances Cheng: She becomes a legend, an idol, but she cannot relate. Frances Cheng: In her eyes, the world is one after another issue to be tackled.;<> Frances Cheng: What she sees as a mere "issue", could be the meaning of their life to others. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Is that a problem? Frances Cheng: How would you feel, if someone attached a label to the most important thing in your life and calls it dispensable?;<> Frances Cheng: Ms. Situ is that kind of person. To her, everything can be traded off, if the price is right. Frances Cheng: That's what I mean by "detached"—seeing everything in terms of value and return. Frances Cheng: The same doesn't apply to the ones who ultimately suffer this loss.;<> ->I think you're overestimating her. Frances Cheng: She is overestimating everyone else. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: She believes we all can see the world as she does. If not, a hearty talk should do the job. <<#Simple(8)>> ->She's not that naïve. Frances Cheng: She's not naïve.;<> Frances Cheng: I guess she just doesn't know her partners well. Thought: Doesn't know them well? Frances Cheng: She expects too much out of them. They're only human.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->You make it sound like she's not human. Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: In a sense, she's more a machine than a human. Thought: Lawrence said something like that, too... ->I know what you mean. Frances Cheng: Yeah. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->Maybe. But what does that have to do with us? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: Honestly, I don't know how it could affect us yet. Frances Cheng: But the foundation of her team isn't as solid as it seems.;<> Frances Cheng: I care about it so much because I was in a similar position once. Frances Cheng: I thought as long as I keep everything in perfect order, my team will agree and follow suit. Frances Cheng: Turns out most of them only felt stressed out.;<> Thought: She's talking about her job. Thought: She's thought a lot in such a short time... Frances Cheng: ...;<> Frances Cheng: *Sigh* Maybe it's just me.;<> Frances Cheng: With so many things happening, I'm all strung up. Especially today, all I could do was wait. <<#Simple(8)>> ->You've had a long day. Frances Cheng: Me? Long day? Frances Cheng: I did nothing other than sitting around, haha. Frances Cheng: You are doing the tough job. ->Yeah, running all over the farm. Frances Cheng: Haha, good exercise. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: You did great. If it were me, I couldn't have infiltrated the farm so smoothly. <<#Simple(8)>> ->I wouldn't call that smoothly. Frances Cheng: Haha, right. Bumps here and there.;<> ->No. You'd do a better job than me. Frances Cheng: Really!?;<> Frances Cheng: I can make a good spy? Thought: She's excited. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yeah. Frances Cheng: Heh! Heh!;<> ->Maybe not. Frances Cheng: Gah!;<> <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: I learned a lot from Ms. Situ about their line of work.;<> Frances Cheng: Nothing of practical value, though. Haha. Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: Phew...;<> Frances Cheng: And. Frances Cheng: We should talk about your body... Thought: Yeah... <<#Simple(8)>> ->Sorry for not telling you sooner. Frances Cheng: It's alright. I understand it can be hard to start the conversation...;<> Frances Cheng: Plus, even if you brought it up, I'd think you're joking. <> ->About genetic engineering? Frances Cheng: ...Yes. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: I wouldn't believe it if not for Ms. Situ... Frances Cheng: That reminded me of those dreams you talked about. <<#Simple(6)>> ->What dreams? Frances Cheng: You forgot? Frances Cheng: For a while, you kept saying you had these lucid dreams. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: Maybe those are not dreams. Could it be...;<> <<#Simple(6)>> ->Your memory while being possessed? Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: Yeah. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: Let's give it a rest. We have more than enough to juggle now.;<> Frances Cheng: Wait till this Vision business is over. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->OK. // ->Don't worry, Fran. Frances Cheng: ...;<> Frances Cheng: I was about to say "how can I not worry". Frances Cheng: But then somehow... Frances Cheng: I actually did feel better, hearing you say it.;<> <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: I was kind of relieved when Ms. Situ revealed your body condition.;<> Frances Cheng: Now at least I know you can handle a fight. Frances Cheng: Hey, speaking of fight, I've never seen you get hurt.;<> Frances Cheng: And you hardly ever get sick... Frances Cheng: Wow... Frances Cheng: It must be nice to be you!;<> Frances Cheng: Haha... Frances Cheng: ...;<> Frances Cheng: *Sigh* Frances Cheng: What have we done to deserve this?;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Don't know. But we can handle it, you and me. Frances Cheng: Ha!;<> Frances Cheng: Well said, Mr. Dai! Frances Cheng: Haha!;<> ->Well, fuck my life. Frances Cheng: I don't know if you're joking or being a defeatist, Mr. Dai. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: Hey, J.;<>;//看著主角 Thought: What? Why stare at me like that? Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: You're a good man.;<> Frances Cheng: After everything, I'm freaking out, really... Frances Cheng: Seeing how calm you are makes me at ease. Frances Cheng: I guess your poker face has an upside, too. Haha! Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: We will get through this, together. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Never doubted it. Frances Cheng: Yes!;<> ->Unless we screw it up, that is. Frances Cheng: Don't jinx it!;<> Frances Cheng: Haha! <> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: Okay. Sleep time. Frances Cheng: 23 says my body needs plenty of rest to heal.;<> Frances Cheng: Good night. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Night. // ->I love you. Frances Cheng: I love you, too. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Thought: I need a shower.;<> <<#End>>

title: 第三日主角內心世界
body: Jason Dai: ...Hey. <<#Simple(8)>> ->I thought I was in the bathroom just one second ago? Jason Dai: I don't know what happened, but my body is now sound asleep. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Shame. I was really looking forward to a hot shower. Jason Dai: ...Sorry to interrupt. ->Neat. Jason Dai: Yeah. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->Hey. ->... <<#EndSimple>> //先判斷 //俊恩:錄取、不錄取 //$UpShelfWrongCount<4 = 錄取 //$UpShelfWrongCount>3 = 不錄取 //阿偉:主角表現好可以錄取、還可以、不好 //$SelectGroupScore>-1 = 錄取 //$SelectGroupScore<0 = 不錄取 <> //if俊恩不錄取+阿偉錄取 <<#If($UpShelfWrongCount>3&&$SelectGroupScore>-1)>> <> <<#EndIf>> //if俊恩錄取+阿偉錄取 <<#If($UpShelfWrongCount<4&&$SelectGroupScore<0)>> <> <<#EndIf>> Jason Dai: So... //IF操作組 <<#If($MCBelongControlDepartment=1)>> Jason Dai: You chose Ops? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->So many things happened today and that is what you care the most about? Jason Dai: If I were you, I'd probably choose the other way. Jason Dai: Thank you all the same. <<#Simple(6)>> ->For what? Jason Dai: For trying to make choices from my perspective. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> Jason Dai: Now, about today's operation. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Honestly, I wouldn't trust Situ too much. Jason Dai: If I said she's reliable, I take it back. Today is one giant mess. Jason Dai: Lawrence was occupied, so she sent me to infiltrate Ops... <<#Simple(12)>> ->For the record, you did agree to help.;<> Jason Dai: I know what I signed up for. The problem is how chaotic their plan is, if you could call that a plan at all. ->Blame it on bad timing. Shit happens.;<> Jason Dai: That's not an excuse for their poor planning. ->Yeah, they're like figuring shits out on the fly.;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: And you. You did what they asked, just like that? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Did you expect me to say no? Jason Dai: ...I did. <<#Simple(10)>> ->How could I have known you're dead against it?;<> Jason Dai: How could I have known you're so easy to manipulate! Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: However. ->I did believe working with them was in our best interest, for now. Jason Dai: Why? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Because the Cellar was under maintenance. It's a great opportunity. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: You're right. ->I thought you'd want this to be over with sooner. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: I do appreciate that. <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: Okay. ->Sorry. Jason Dai: No need. What happened, happened. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: I understand you didn't have any better choice under such circumstances. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Although... ->Don't blame me.;<> Jason Dai: I'm not trying to— Jason Dai: *Sigh* Jason Dai: ...
