Jason Dai: Why? <<@解釋為啥代替張隆生去地窖調查>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Because I don't think there's any real danger.;<> Jason Dai: No real danger? What if you're caught? Jason Dai: And you were caught, didn't you? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->So I was. Still, there was no real danger. Jason Dai: Because of sheer luck. You didn't know that before. ->No pain, no gain, Jason Dai. Jason Dai: ... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: Let's put the infiltration mission aside for now. Jason Dai: There is one bigger issue. ->Because you grabbed control and agreed to help them in the first place. Jason Dai: I know what I said, but— Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: ...OK. You're right. Jason Dai: Their operation would stretch for a couple of days if not for you. Jason Dai: But... ->Because it helps to get your and Fran's life back on track sooner. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: ...OK. You're right. Jason Dai: Their operation would stretch for a couple of days if not for you. Jason Dai: But... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: One way or another, Situ and her gang lied to us. <<#Simple(8)>> ->It's more like not telling the whole truth?;<> Jason Dai: The result is the same. Jason Dai: They intentionally misled us. ->Yeah, I agree.;<> ->By which you mean? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: This whole operation is conducted without OWL's approval. Jason Dai: In other words, it's illegal. <<#Simple(10)>> ->They agreed to take the responsibility if they're caught.;<> Jason Dai: So they say. Jason Dai: If OWL is really serious about it, Fran and I won't walk away untouched. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Be realistic. You're against Vision. No one walks away untouched.;<> Jason Dai: That may be the case, but... ->Yeah, right. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->I don't see illegal operation as a big deal.;<> Jason Dai: Because it's not you taking the fall. Jason Dai: At the end of the day, who's the owner of this body, huh? ->They intentionally talked us into this.;<> Jason Dai: Exactly. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: They concealed the truth for their own interest, and we're paying for it. <<#Simple(8)>> ->They helped you and Fran in the process.;<> Jason Dai: ...I'm well aware of that. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Isn't that a win-win partnership?;<> Jason Dai: Depends on the outcome. Jason Dai: If they screw up for not having OWL's blessing... Jason Dai: Then we have a lose-lose partnership. ->Stop bitching, man.;<>;<> Jason Dai: ... ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->To them, we're just pawns.;<> Jason Dai: ...Pawns that are dispensable, when the situation is right. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: I do believe they are fighting Vision or even OWL for a legitimate cause. Jason Dai: And I do believe they're trying to do good and uphold justice... Jason Dai: But I don't want to be one of them do-gooders, or rub Vision the wrong way. <<#Simple(10)>> ->Fran has been rubbing Vision the wrong way for a while, in case you haven't noticed.;<> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: I... <<#Simple(8)>> ->And we know they're targeting Vision from the beginning.;<> ->We can't protect Fran without making an enemy of Vision. Don't you agree?;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: ...You're right. Jason Dai: Although I thought they were backed by somebody. Jason Dai: To find out they're on their own is shocking, to say at least. ->Why did you agree to cooperate with them, then? Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: I... <<#Simple(8)>> ->We know they're targeting Vision from the very beginning, no?;<> ->We can't protect Fran without making an enemy of Vision. Don't you agree?;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: ...You're right. Jason Dai: Although I thought they were backed by somebody. Jason Dai: To find out they're on their own is shocking, to say at least. ->But they dragged you into this mission against Vision.;<> Jason Dai: ...Can't say they dragged me. Jason Dai: They did put Fran and me in a very awkward position, though. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: Say... Jason Dai: We don't know if they really care about Fran and I. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Man, I hate seeing the situation clearly and not being able to do anything about it... Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: What do you say? Shall we continue to trust Situ? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yeah, we should.;<> //if前面特支持 <<#If($TrustSituOrNot>3)>> Jason Dai: Right. You've been on their side all along. <<#EndIf>> //if前面特反對 <<#If($TrustSituOrNot<-2)>> Jason Dai: I thought you'd say no, given your attitude so far and all. <<#EndIf>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Don't you think they're just using us? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I don't mind, as long as our interests are aligned. Jason Dai: ...Aligned interests, huh. Jason Dai: What if we're no longer aligned? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->That's a discussion for another day. Jason Dai: I see. ->I believe they won't turn against us without a warning. Jason Dai: I hope so. <<#EndSimple>> ->I don't. She's genuinely trying to help us. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: I'll trust you on that, since you know her better. Jason Dai: I've been only watching on the sidelines, after all. ->Do you have a choice?;<> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Shut down our brain and go with the flow? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yeah. ->I was trying to say whatever we think won't change things. Jason Dai: That's... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Never mind. Jason Dai: I don't want to agree with you, but you're right. <<#EndSimple>> ->Right. They may hurt us.;<> //if前面特支持 <<#If($TrustSituOrNot>3)>> Jason Dai: I thought you'd trust Situ. Jason Dai: You've been on her side all along, after all. <<#GoTo(@跳過一段主角問玩家怎麼說)>> <<#EndIf>> Jason Dai: What makes you think that? <<@跳過一段主角問玩家怎麼說>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->They value their objectives over everything, us included. Jason Dai: Yeah, I have the same concern... Jason Dai: If sacrificing us helps to get the dirt on Vision, maybe they will... ->They don't have what it takes to win this fight. Jason Dai: Once they lose, whether to Vision or to OWL, we go down with them. Jason Dai: By "we", I also mean Fran. <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: As of now, though, we have to deal with the reality... Jason Dai: We have no choice but to work with her. <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: Say... Jason Dai: Fran seems to trust them. Jason Dai: She's worried about their internal dynamics, though. Jason Dai: I'm not worrying too much about that. Jason Dai: What about you? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Fran is right. Jason Dai: How so? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Lawrence isn't very reliable. Jason Dai: Yeah. Jason Dai: I'm surprised that Situ is working with him at all. ->Situ is kind of heartless. Jason Dai: Agreed. Jason Dai: But for a mission like this, being heartless should be a plus, no? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Maybe. ->A plus for lone wolf operations. We are now moving as a team. ->Her team members won't think so. <<#EndSimple>> ->23's romance issue is a risk. Jason Dai: The reason being? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->No reason, just a feeling. ->You don't mix personal feelings into an operation like this. <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: Yeah... ->I don't think anything. None of my business. ->She's worrying too much. Jason Dai: ...Is that so? <<#EndSimple>> //IF有管隆生。 <<#If($Ron3NightEnd=1)>> Jason Dai: Speaking of Lawrence, you had quite a talk with him. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Sort of. ->And you don't find it agreeable? <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: As before, I don't want you to be too involved when it comes to the time to pick sides. <<#Simple(8)>> ->I know what you mean. Jason Dai: Yeah. ->Pick sides? Jason Dai: I mean, making friends with Lawrence is generally OK. Jason Dai: But do keep a minimum distance... Jason Dai: If this team of theirs implodes someday, don't stand right next to it. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Honestly, I don't think Situ should have him on the team. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Why talking about this, all of a sudden? ->That so? Jason Dai: Forthright. Impulsive. Idolizing what seems good. Turning a blind eye to their ugly side. Jason Dai: His whole world tumbles down when he sees the idol's feet of clay. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: By that I mean, he's trying too hard to impress Situ. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Agreed. Jason Dai: Yeah. ->Why do you mind that so much? Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Because I used to deal with people like him. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: And. Jason Dai: If they ask me to do risky stuff again, just say yes. Jason Dai: Will you? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Sure thing. // ->I thought you didn't like risky stuff? Jason Dai: I've changed my mind. Jason Dai: If risky stuff is inevitable, I might as well take the initiative. Jason Dai: Get it over with faster, and find out what we're really dealing with while I'm at it. Jason Dai: I have a feeling that Situ will lie to us again. <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Come to think of it, even if Situ is 100% trustworthy... Jason Dai: Our body is still a problem. OWL will hunt us down. Jason Dai: Looks like our only way out is to join OWL... Jason Dai: ... <<#End>>;//主角崩潰

title: 基地討論核心事件
body: //司徒寧走出來，淡入淡出到桌子前 23: What does he say?;<> Nin Situ: He says he'll take care of it. 23: Hm...;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Who were you calling? Nin Situ: An old friend. ->Take care of what? Nin Situ: Clean up. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: Now Vision won't be able to track you down based on what you left at the Flop Core.;<> Lawrence Chang: How could you be so sure? Nin Situ: Because I made sure Yuna Hsu is in charge of the Flop Core investigation.;<> Lawrence Chang: Huh? 23: Chill. You're not supposed to understand adult business.;<>;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: Hey!;<>;<> Nin Situ: Lawrence. Are you sure you were assaulted by mercenaries?;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: Yeah.;<> Nin Situ: What's your opinion, Jason?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Definitely not security guards, for sure. ->They are in a uniform of sorts, and face masks. <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: Grey uniform, and masks like skull faces?;<>;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: Right! How do you know? Nin Situ: ...;<> 23: ...;<> 23: I know who they are, then.;<> Nin Situ: It's a mercenary group made of wanted criminals.;<>;<> Nin Situ: The leader codename Chameleon, is ex-military.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->How do you know? You haven't seen them. // ->Vision is hiring criminals? 23: If you're doing illegal things, you might as well do it with like-minded people. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: ...We have seen Chameleon before. Lawrence Chang: In other missions? Nin Situ: That, too. Nin Situ: I mean, we saw him here.;<>;//開啟投影，停在白壁虎舉槍的瞬間 Nin Situ: This shooter. He is Chameleon. Nin Situ: It was just a suspicion. But after what you said... Nin Situ: Now I'm certain Vision is working with him.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Why didn't you say so before? Nin Situ: Because it was no more than a suspicion. Thought: Well, you could have said you had a suspicion... ->Anything we need to know about him? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: He's notoriously aggressive. Caused lots of trouble for OWL. Nin Situ: Hiring him means Vision has more at stake than we thought.;<>;<> 23: I'm monitoring Vision's internal communications. Looks like employees, no matter the rank, are not aware of these mercenaries.;<> 23: They are keeping it a secret from their own people. Thought: That explains why Hsiao was told to leave without handing over.;<>;<> Nin Situ: ...;<> Nin Situ: It's a tough day for you two.;<>;<>;<>;//關閉投影 Nin Situ: Especially you, Jason.;<> Nin Situ: Sorry for putting you through this. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Don't be. I'm well prepared. Lawrence Chang: Cool as ever, Jason! ->Just make sure this doesn't happen again.;<>;//這會記錄 Nin Situ: I will try. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: You did exceptionally well to get out unaided. Lawrence Chang: All thanks to my grenade! Right, Jason?;<>;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Yeah, great timing. Lawrence Chang: Heh. ->You should have used it sooner. Lawrence Chang: Oh, sorry... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: You each took down a henchman of Chameleon's. I'm surprised.;<>;<>;<> Nin Situ: They are professional hitmen with abundant field experience, after all.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->One of them took me down, though. ->Until we met the last guy, that is. Lawrence Chang: Yeah! ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: That guy's on a whole different level. Super strong. We could barely touch him. him.;<>;<> Nin Situ: Military grade exoskeleton suit?;<> Lawrence Chang: Pretty sure he had it. Nin Situ: ...;<>;<>;<>;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->What's military grade exoskeleton suit? ->What does it do? Lawrence Chang: Huh? I thought you'd know better than anyone else, Jason. Lawrence Chang: Haven't you seen it in the army? Thought: He's right. I even tried it on a few times... Nin Situ: Quick explanation. Nin Situ: Exoskeleton suits are a kind of enhancement gear for protection and ability boost.;<> Lawrence Chang: Some dock workers wear them for heavy lifting. The same thing!;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: The civilian version only increases strength, no protection or speed boost.;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: I used to wear it on one of my gig jobs!;<> Lawrence Chang: It's no match for the military grade, though. Lawrence Chang: I mean, both the performance and the price tag...;<>;<> <>;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> 23: If Chameleon's men are armed with those...;<>;<> 23: Vision must be paying them some serious money. Nin Situ: Right.;<> Nin Situ: ...;<> Nin Situ: 23, when can we have the analysis result of the Flop Core data? 23: Noon tomorrow, the soonest.;<>;<> 23: Once done, we will be able to see what exactly is happening in the Cellar. 23: I mean, we have all the data that can be had. The truth has to be in it somewhere.;<> 23: ...Finally, the moment of truth.;<> Nin Situ: Yeah...;<> 23: Although we really have nothing to do before that.;<> Lawrence Chang: Hey, Nin.;<>;<>;<> Nin Situ: Yes? Lawrence Chang: Can you give me a copy of the data Jason retrieved? Nin Situ: What do you need it for? Lawrence Chang: Just want to help. An extra pair of eyes doesn't hurt, eh? 23: Are you doubting me, boy? Lawrence Chang: N-no! I'm trying to help in any way I can.;<> 23: I don't mind. You're not stupid enough to share it with someone else, are you?;<> Lawrence Chang: Of course not! 23: What do you say, Nin? Shall I give him a copy?;<>;<>;<> Nin Situ: ...;<> Nin Situ: No. Lawrence Chang: Huh? Why?;<>;<>;<> Nin Situ: This is highly classified information. Lawrence Chang: But— Nin Situ: No means no. Lawrence Chang: ...;<> Lawrence Chang: Okay.;<> Thought: Lawrence doesn't look very convinced. 23: Okay, everyone!;<>;<> 23: That wraps the business part up.;<>;<> 23: Dinner time?;<> Nin Situ: Take Lawrence to dinner. I need a moment with Jason and Frances.;<>;<>;<> 23: ... 23: Let's go, Lawrence.;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: Oh...;<>;<> 23: Show some spirit, man! Dinner's on me. Lawrence Chang: Hell yeah! You mean, for real?;<> 23: You deserve a treat. Let's go. Lawrence Chang: Yay!;<> //隆生根23離開 <<#End>>

title: 司徒跟主角程芳語重心長
body: //運鏡要拉近 Nin Situ: You've been silent, Frances. Are you feeling alright?;<>;<> Frances Cheng: Me...!?;<> Frances Cheng: Oh, I'm alright. Thought: I can tell something's on her mind. Nin Situ: Good. Nin Situ: There are a few things I'd like to go over with you.;<> Nin Situ: But first of all... //司徒寧低頭， Nin Situ: Please accept my apology. I didn't intend to put Jason in danger.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->It's not a big deal. I know what I signed up for. Nin Situ: No. //if有拜託不要再讓自己陷入危險 <<#If($ToldSiTuDontWantDangerous=true)>> Nin Situ: You said this better not happen again. <<#EndIf>> Nin Situ: I could have avoided the situation today. ->This is pointless. What happened, happened. Nin Situ: It has a point to me. Nin Situ: Examining past mistakes is a critical part of my job. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: That said, I have a question for you, Jason.;<> Nin Situ: You ran into a security guard in the Flop Core. Did he say anything?;<> Thought: Hsiao said quite a lot of things...;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Anything, like? ->He did mention stuff. <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: Did he say why he was stationed there? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->No progress. Nin Situ: ...;<> ->He was sent there on short notice. Nin Situ: I see.;<> <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: Did he mention how much they know? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Know about what? Nin Situ: About our plan to infiltrate the Flop Core.;<>;<> Nin Situ: Was it a general plan to elevate security all around the farm? Nin Situ: Or are they waiting for us specifically?;<>;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I think they knew we were coming. <<#GoTo(@吼司徒共他知道會有人來。)>> ->I'm not sure. ->I don't think they know anything. Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: Really? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Okay, he did say something weird. <<#GoTo(@吼司徒共他知道會有人來。)>> ->You don't believe me? Nin Situ: No. I was surprised that my hunch was completely off. <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> ->They knew someone's coming to the Flop Core. <<@吼司徒共他知道會有人來。>> Frances Cheng: Really? Thought: Fran look very surprised.;<> Frances Cheng: Doesn't that mean our operation was compromised? Nin Situ: I have this concern.;<> Nin Situ: Did the guard specifically mention "inspector"?;<> Thought: Hsiao did say that...;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yes, they knew about that, too. Nin Situ: ... ->No. <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: I see. <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: Last question. Did Lawrence propose to enter the Flop Core alone?;<> //if答應過隆生不會說 <<#If(PromiseRonWontTellSiTu=true)>> Thought: I promised him to keep it a secret... <<#EndIf>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->He did. // ->No. <> <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: I see. Frances Cheng: Why ask? Thought: Fran?;<> Nin Situ: Anything wrong? Frances Cheng: I'm curious. Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: That is not relevant. Frances Cheng: But— <> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Give her a break, Fran.;<> // ->If it's irrelevant, it's not classified. <<@追問司徒感麻問隆生是否獨自進入>> <> Nin Situ: ...;<> Nin Situ: I guarantee it has nothing to do with either of you. Frances Cheng: I wouldn't insist if it's classified, Ms. Situ.;<> Frances Cheng: But if it's not... <<#Simple(8)>> ->We have the right to know. ->You need to create transparency as much as possible. <> Nin Situ: ...I see. Nin Situ: I'm asking about Lawrence because I'm worried about him.;<> Frances Cheng: Worried about him? Nin Situ: I didn't intend to include him in this mission, because...;<> <<#Simple(12)>> ->He lacks experience. Nin Situ: No. Lawrence may look clueless sometimes for sure...;<>;<> Nin Situ: But he's a very reliable helper. We have worked on many cases together. Nin Situ: I trust his capabilities.;<> Nin Situ: However... ->He holds a personal grudge against the flop farming industry.;//在跟隆生下地窖電梯時有問到它對運算產業的看法 Nin Situ: ...You're very perceptive.;<>;<> Thought: She is genuinely surprised by my response. Nin Situ: That is correct.;<> ->He can't keep a secret. Nin Situ: No. That is groundless concern.;<>;<> Nin Situ: Lawrence is a good boy. He knows when to keep his mouth shut. Nin Situ: However...;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: Because of what happened to him, Lawrence hates everything related to flop farming with a passion.;<> Nin Situ: I was worried that he may take this too personally.;<> Nin Situ: As a general rule, personal feelings are counterproductive on missions. Nin Situ: Moreover, I don't want to see him hurt.;<> <<#Simple(6)>> ->Hurt? How so? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: He's the kind of young man who believes every problem has a perfect solution if you try hard enough.;<> Nin Situ: All cases we worked on did have perfect solutions. But this time...;<> Nin Situ: This time we're against Vision. Nin Situ: We must be prepared to face less than ideal results.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Less than ideal? Nin Situ: That we may not be able to stop Vision, or make them pay.;<> Nin Situ: In that case, we must find a Plan B for you two.;<> Nin Situ: No matter how this goes, "taking Vision down" is unrealistic.;<> Thought: She's right. It's a very real possibility...;<> ->I understand. Nin Situ: Right. We have lived long enough to see reality as it is. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: There are situations that you have to admit defeat. Nin Situ: Give up now, for a chance to try again another day. Nin Situ: To most people, this is the common sense, I believe.;<> <<#Simple(6)>> ->What if I choose to stand my ground, no matter what? Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: Depends.;<>;<> Nin Situ: Maybe you have a reason to keep on fighting to the bitter end. Nin Situ: Or maybe you're seeing the inevitable defeat as a cheap way out.;<>;<> Thought: Fran is momentarily stunned... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: For you two, surrender might be the more sensible way to go. Nin Situ: Your priority is to put your life back on track.;<> Thought: She glanced at Fran. Nin Situ: At least, Jason, you'd appreciate this option.;<>;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Should I be mad at that? Nin Situ: Sorry, I meant no offense.;<>;<> Nin Situ: There is nothing morally wrong with surrendering. Nin Situ: All I meant to say is that you don't have a reason to fight Vision to the death.;<>;<> ->You think you know me, huh? Nin Situ: I wouldn't say that.;<>;<> Nin Situ: This is based on what I know about you. Nin Situ: All I meant to say is that you don't have a reason to fight Vision to the death.;<>;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: Lawrence is a different case. To him, this is a holy war. We must raze Vision's evil empire to the ground. Frances Cheng: I think I know what you mean... Frances Cheng: But why did you ask whether he went into the Flop Core alone?;<> Nin Situ: I have a feeling that he may want to prepare some secret weapon of his own. Nin Situ: I denied his request to see the data for the same concern.;<> Nin Situ: If he goes renegade and engages Vision on his own with this data, things will be even more out of control. Nin Situ: Also, you both know that 23 will mind.;<> Thought: ...She means legitimate process and privacy protection. Nin Situ: I don't want to disappoint 23, or risk Lawrence.;<> Nin Situ: That's why I'm asking you, Jason. Nin Situ: Sorry for keeping you in the dark.;<> //if剛剛沒說 <<#If($LieAboutRonDindntGoByHimself=true)>> Thought: Shall I tell her...? <<#Simple(8)>> ->Lawrence did enter the Flop Core alone. Nin Situ: I see...;<>;<> Nin Situ: Why didn't you tell me before? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Now I know you care about him. ->It feels like good timing. ->He asked me not to tell. Nin Situ: Ah, I see.;<> Nin Situ: Thank you. <<#EndSimple>> <> ->... <<#EndSimple>> <> <<#EndIf>> Frances Cheng: It must hard to be you, Ms. Situ. Frances Cheng: Leading a team like this, there must be a million things to juggle. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Especially when you have a team like this. Nin Situ: Don't say that.;<> Thought: She's smiling, meaning she agrees. <> ->Well, it's part of her job. Frances Cheng: That's mean, J!;<> Nin Situ: No, he's right. <> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: I chose this job. I try to do it as best as I can. Frances Cheng: I admire you. Nin Situ: The feeling is mutual. I admire you, too. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Ahem. Shall I make myself scarce for a bit? Nin Situ: Ha, no need.;<>;<> Thought: She appreciates the joke. <> ->Anybody admires me? ->I admire you both. Nin Situ: ...;<> Frances Cheng: ...;<> <<#Simple(6)>> ->Hey! ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: Haha! Nin Situ: Haha. <> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: ...;<> //這裡結束 <<#GoTo(@隆生幹嘛一人進去了解事件結束)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: Enough. Nin Situ: So much on this subject. Frances Cheng: ... Nin Situ: ... Frances Cheng: Okay. //if剛剛選了「芳，別問了吧。」就近不來，或是都已經進追問了也進不來 <<#If($DLStateFloat=0)>> <<#Simple(6)>> ->No! You can't keep things from us! <<#GoTo(@追問司徒感麻問隆生是否獨自進入)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> <<@隆生幹嘛一人進去了解事件結束>> Nin Situ: There is one thing you must know. Thought: She suddenly got a whole lot more serious... Nin Situ: What I'm about to say will decide the outlook of your future. I mean you both.;<> //司徒把手抬起展示手上的黑樁手甲。 <> Nin Situ: This spike is my hovering gadget. I can control it within a certain range. range.;<>;<> Frances Cheng: Control it? Nin Situ: Yes. Shoot it out as a weapon and retrieve in a split second. Nin Situ: It also serves as a fulcrum for acrobatic maneuvers.;<> Frances Cheng: Wow...;<> Frances Cheng: Hovering technology can be weaponized? Nin Situ: The technology was developed for weapons. Civilian applications are a byproduct.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Cooool. ->But what does it have to do with us. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: Could you fetch me a bottle of tea, Jason?;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Huh? We're talking about tea now? ->Here is Jason Dai, everyone's favorite tea boy. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: Thank you. Thought: The way she says it, she's not asking... <<#End>> //主角下椅子，玩家需要自己向右走，到點的十後停住主角撥放司徒寧突然射出黑棒，主角瞬間反應抓住黑棒

title: 熱炒店聊天2
body:
Lawrence Chang: Heh heh...;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_便服2_熱炒看內返回)>>
<<#Simple(6)>>
->What?
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Lawrence Chang: Tell you something...;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_便服_熱炒右看)>>
Lawrence Chang: 23 said telltale signs of liars, right?;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_便服2_熱炒吃兩口)>>
<<#Simple(8)>>
->Yeah, you scratch your head.
    Lawrence Chang: No, I'm not talking about me!
->So?
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Lawrence Chang: Sometimes she can't help herself.
Lawrence Chang: Like when she's really nervous, or pissed drunk.;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_便服2_熱炒夾一口)>>
<<#Simple(8)>>
->You mean, she just lied?
    Lawrence Chang: Yep!
->How did you know?
    Lawrence Chang: I have sharp eyes!
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Lawrence Chang: I figured out that her telltale sign is touching her neck.;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_便服_熱炒右看返回)>>
Lawrence Chang: Remember when I asked if she's OK with being just Nin's colleague?;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_便服2_熱炒吃一口)>>
<<#Simple(8)>>
->Uh-huh.
    //
->That was not a very nice question.
    Lawrence Chang: Okay! Point is!
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Lawrence Chang: When she answered, she touched her neck!;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_便服_熱炒右看)>>
<<#Simple(8)>>
->So?
    Lawrence Chang: So she really wants to be with Nin as a couple!
->This is not funny.
    Lawrence Chang: No!
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Lawrence Chang: I'd totally root for her! She and Nin belong together!;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_便服_熱炒右看吃飯)>>
<<Set($DLStateFloat,0)>>
<<#Simple(10)>>
->Don't meddle with other people's relationship.
    Lawrence Chang: 23 is not trying!;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_便服2_熱炒吃兩口)>>
    Lawrence Chang: How could she get there if she doesn't even try!
->Good for you.
    //
->I don't think she's getting anywhere.;<<Set($DLStateFloat,1)>>
    Lawrence Chang: Not you, too!;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_便服2_熱炒吃兩口)>>
    Lawrence Chang: Don't be a quitter!
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Lawrence Chang: Want to give her a push, Jason?
Lawrence Chang: I mean, after this operation.
<<#Simple(8)>>
->Uh-huh.
    Lawrence Chang: Hey! I'm asking seriously!
->Sure. Let me know what I can do.
    Lawrence Chang: Great!
    //if剛剛說不會成功
    <<#If($DLStateFloat=1)>>
        Lawrence Chang: Great! For a second I thought you wouldn't agree.
    <<#EndIf>>
->No. I don't want to meddle.
    Lawrence Chang: Okay...
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Lawrence Chang: Eh?;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_便服2_熱炒夾兩口)>>
Lawrence Chang: ...Hold on. I think I forgot something.;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_便服_熱炒右看返回)>>
<<#Simple(8)>>
->Like what?
->Like, 23 said dinner's on her?
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Lawrence Chang: Aaaahhh! Right! She left without paying!;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_便服_熱炒右看)>>
Lawrence Chang: Damn...;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_便服2_熱炒吃一口)>>
<<#Simple(8)>>
->Not a big deal. Dinner's on me.
->Let's split the bill.
    Lawrence Chang: No! I can't let you pay for it.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Lawrence Chang: I'll go check out, then ask her for reimbursement later.
<<#Simple(8)>>
->Thanks.
    Lawrence Chang: Don't mention it.
->Maybe she'll forget when she sobers up.
    Lawrence Chang: Right! She does that all the time!
    Lawrence Chang: I won't let her go so easily this time!
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Lawrence Chang: We're about done. Shall we go back?
<<#Simple(99999)>>
->Let's go.
    //
->I'm not so eager to go back yet.
    Lawrence Chang: Good.
<<#EndSimple>>
Lawrence Chang: I'm gonna get a cup of bubble tea. Then we can find a corner to talk.;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_便服_熱炒右看返回)>>
<<#Simple(8)>>
->Now?
    Lawrence Chang: Yep.
->Why don't we just talk here?
    Lawrence Chang: They won't let us hog the table forever, haha...
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Lawrence Chang: Shall we? There are things I'd like to talk in private.;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_便服_熱炒右看)>>
<<#Simple(99999)>>
->Sure.
    <<@答應隆生喝手搖哩哩哩>>
    Lawrence Chang: Great.
->Sorry, I need some rest.
    Lawrence Chang: Huh...
    Lawrence Chang: Okay.
    <<#Simple(99999)>>
    ->OK, let's grab some bubble tea, then.
        <<#GoTo(@答應隆生喝手搖哩哩哩)>>
    ->Sorry, I'm really exhausted.
        Lawrence Chang: Right. You need a break.;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_便服_熱炒右看點頭)>>
    <<#EndSimple>>
    Lawrence Chang: Good night.
    <<#Simple(6)>>
    ->Night.
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
    //if麵店
    <<#If($ToldWoodSeaChangStory=2)>>
        Thought: I have a meeting appointment.
    <<#EndIf>>
    //隆生走去
    <<Set($DLState,不跟隆生喝飲料)>>
    <<#End>>
<<#EndSimple>>
Lawrence Chang: Will you go get the bubble tea first? I'm gonna pay the check.
<<#Simple(8)>>
->No problem.
    Lawrence Chang: Thanks!;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_便服_熱炒右看點頭)>>
->Don't forget about the bubble tea money later.
    Lawrence Chang: Sure.;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_便服_熱炒右看點頭)>>
->What would you like?
    Lawrence Chang: Oh, right.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Lawrence Chang: Medium nata jelly bubble tea, no ice, half sugar, extra bubbles for topping.
<<#Simple(8)>>
->OK.
    //
->Come again?
    Lawrence Chang: Medium nata jelly bubble tea, no ice, half sugar, extra bubbles for topping.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Lawrence Chang: See you at the betel nut joint!
<<PlayUntil(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_便服_熱炒右看返回)>>
<<#End>>;//隆生去結帳，結帳那編排很多人她在排隊

title: 第三日主角內心世界3
body: Jason Dai: ... <<#Simple(6)>> ->What? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: I just... Jason Dai: ... //if好感度過低 <<#If($RelationshipWithMC<-3)>> Jason Dai: You have something against me? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I do. Jason Dai: Figured. ->What do you mean? Jason Dai: Maybe it's just me, but I have this feeling that you don't like me much. <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: It's been a while since we first met. //IF不願意配合 <<#If($HelpMCEnding=1)>> Jason Dai: You've been uncooperative off the bat. <<#EndIf>> Jason Dai: And I can't call you friendly ever since. Jason Dai: Whatever problem you have, say it. <> <<@主角問玩家對自己有啥意見是不是>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I'm working my ass off on your behalf, and you keep complaining. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: You're right. Jason Dai: You could have fucked up my life royally if you want... Jason Dai: But so far, you've been working hard to keep things going. Jason Dai: However, I don't think I'm complaining. Jason Dai: I'm sharing with you my concerns and plans. Jason Dai: If you take it as pointing fingers... Jason Dai: I never intended to. Jason Dai: Hopefully you will understand. <<#Simple(12)>> ->In that case, I do understand. Jason Dai: Yeah. ->Intended or not, I take offense all the same. ->No can do. I just don't like you as a person. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Fair enough. Jason Dai: We don't have to love each other. Jason Dai: You don't like me, I don't trust you. Jason Dai: We're even. <<#EndSimple>> ->No. Maybe you just read me wrong. ($DLStateFloat) <> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Sorry, but I don't think it's as simple as reading you wrong. Jason Dai: Let me ask again. Do you have anything against me? <> ->I just don't like you as a person. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Fair enough. Jason Dai: We don't have to love each other. Jason Dai: You don't like me, I don't trust you. Jason Dai: We're even. <<#EndSimple>> <<#GoTo(@第三日內心世界收尾)>> <<#EndIf>> Jason Dai: Sorry. Jason Dai: I didn't realize until you pointed out. Jason Dai: I've been complaining all the time, about this and that, about you. Jason Dai: You could have fucked up my life royally if you want... //IF第一日內心世界很抗局幫主角 <<#If($HelpMCEnding=1)>> Jason Dai: Actually... Jason Dai: You've been very uncooperative since we first met. <<#EndIf>> Jason Dai: But so far, you've been working hard to keep things going. Jason Dai: In that case... Jason Dai: I shouldn't apologize. Jason Dai: I owe you a thank you. Jason Dai: Thank you. <<#Simple(8)>> ->I did what I wanted to. ->Don't mention it. Jason Dai: Haha... ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<@第三日內心世界收尾>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: We must talk about Woody Chen, too... Jason Dai: ...Don't you agree? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Not Woody Chen. He's an MBU. Jason Dai: ...You're right. ->What's there's to talk about? Jason Dai: You have nothing to talk about? Really? <<#EndSimple>> //if陳木海事件有完成 <<#If($HadWoodSeaChangTalk=0)>> Jason Dai: If what they say is true... Jason Dai: Our problem won't have an immediate solution. Jason Dai: Finding this Source, whatever it is, can't be easy... Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Damn... <<#Simple(8)>> ->Sorry, Jason. Jason Dai: No, it's not your fault. ->There's always the possibility that the problem solves itself. Jason Dai: True... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Although... Jason Dai: What this "kin" of ours said, about the figure that killed your creator... Jason Dai: What do you make of that? Jason Dai: Observing you? Is that really what it wants? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Maybe? Jason Dai: ... ->I don't know enough to tell yet. Jason Dai: Yeah. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: ...You met it in person, right? Jason Dai: Did it attempt to communicate? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->It did say something. Jason Dai: Like what? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->It said I must prove my mind...? ->It said this is my and someone else's blah blah...? ->It said I'm free...? Jason Dai: Cryptic. Jason Dai: But I feel that... Jason Dai: It doesn't mean you harm...? <<#Simple(10)>> ->I don't know. ->Probably. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->Don't remember. Jason Dai: ...That so? Jason Dai: No can do, then. <<#EndSimple>> ->No. <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: ... //if關係不好 <<#If($RelationshipWithMC<-3)>> Jason Dai: Never mind. Jason Dai: Don't dwell on it if we can't do a thing about it. Jason Dai: We will be separated when the time comes. Jason Dai: That much is for sure. <<#GoTo(@跳過主角說要幫玩家找真身)>> <<#EndIf>> //if關係好 Jason Dai: Hey, I've been wanting to ask you this... Jason Dai: Do you want me to go looking for you? <<#Simple(6)>> ->Huh? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: Based on what we know, to separate us, finding the Source is a must. Jason Dai: There's a very real possibility that you will be gone afterwards. Won't you agree? <<#Simple(10)>> ->Yes. // ->Isn't that what you're going for? Jason Dai: No, I don't mean that. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: I'm just thinking... Jason Dai: Suppose you're gone. Would you like me to go looking for you? Jason Dai: To find out what you are, and maybe... Jason Dai: Maybe set you free. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Why would you want to do that? Jason Dai: As I said, you could have fucked up my life but chose not to. Jason Dai: I owe you that much. ->How could you do it? Jason Dai: Don't know, but I can find out. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: I can't promise any specific result, or that I'll risk my life for it... Jason Dai: With a reasonable range, I will try my best. Jason Dai: What do you say? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->That would be great. Jason Dai: We have a deal, then. Jason Dai: Once you're out of my body, I'll look for you. ->I won't want to trouble you with that. Jason Dai: ...I see. Jason Dai: If that's the case, forget I asked. <<#EndSimple>> <<@跳過主角說要幫玩家找真身>> <<#EndIf>> //if陳木海事件沒有完成 <<#If($HadWoodSeaChangTalk=-1)>> //if沒對陳木海說自己的遭遇 <<#If($ToldWoodSeaChangStory=-1)>> Jason Dai: He is the only one who could potentially solve our problem. Jason Dai: And you chose not to work with him... Jason Dai: Why? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I don't trust him. ->I think he means us harm. <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: But we can't do anything on our own... Jason Dai: It's too late, though. Jason Dai: Didn't he say we will never see him again? <<#EndIf>> //if有說遭遇 <<#If($ToldWoodSeaChangStory>-1)>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Why didn't you go to the meeting? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I forgot. Jason Dai: Forgot...? ->I sensed danger. Jason Dai: He could find the answer we need. That justifies risks. <<#Simple(8)>> ->In hindsight, you could be right. ->Sorry, I beg to differ. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Did he promise to share information even if we're no show? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I think he did. ->Only what he wants to share. <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: Yeah. Jason Dai: Let's wait for him to make contact. //if如果有告訴陳木海屠殺而且沒跟主角說過屠殺 <<#If($DayOneTellMCErrorKill=-1&&$ToldWoodSeaErrorStory=0)>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: When talking to him, you mentioned witnessing an attack. Jason Dai: Why didn't you tell me before? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I didn't see the need to. ->Would that make a difference? <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Never mind. Forget about it. <<#EndIf>> <<#EndIf>> <<#EndIf>> <<#Wait(2)>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: ...Yeah. <<#Simple(8)>> ->What's up? // ->We're out of time? Jason Dai: Yeah. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: The body is about to wake up. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Anything else, before we go? Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Yes. ->See you next time. Jason Dai: Wait. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: ...Don't let Fran know that I'm possessed. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Roger that. Jason Dai: Thanks. ->Why? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: She must be scared, knowing that I can be possessed. Jason Dai: I don't want her to worry for me. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: I'll leave the rest to you. <<#Simple(8)>> ->You can count on me. ->I'll try my best. ->It's not like you have any other option, man. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#End>>

title: 第三日主角內心世界2
body:
Jason Dai: ...
Jason Dai: I was naïve enough to believe that...
Jason Dai: If we bust Shane Yan, we could call it a day.
Jason Dai: Then Yuna Hsu happened, followed by OWL, genetic engineering, the Flop Core...
Jason Dai: ...
Jason Dai: We are not dealing with one person or event.
Jason Dai: This is a whole interconnected, holistic mess.
<<#Simple(8)>>
->What are you talking about?
    //
->You need a break, dude.
    Jason Dai: No, I don't.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Jason Dai: What Fran and I have seen is only a tiny part of it.
Jason Dai: This is a giant conspiracy that has been going on for a long time.
Jason Dai: Situ is not the first to discover it.
Jason Dai: She's just the first to stand up and do something about it.
<<#Simple(8)>>
->Don't we know that all along?
    Jason Dai: We do, but I didn't know what it implies until now.
->Are you saying Situ is a good person?
    Jason Dai: Can't say if she's good or bad yet.
    Jason Dai: But she's got some balls for sure.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Jason Dai: Where would we be if she never showed up?
Jason Dai: Fran would be dead, and I couldn't do a thing about it.
Jason Dai: I wouldn't even know what's happening.
<<#Simple(8)>>
->And you're trying to say?
    //
->You were pretty judgmental about her not so long ago.
    //if玩家剛剛也嘴司徒
    <<#If($TrustSituOrNot<-2)>>
        Jason Dai: And you were no better.
        //if後來選信任
        <<#If($DecideTrustSituOrNot=1)>>
            <<#Simple(6)>>
            ->I did say we could trust her, though.
                Jason Dai: That you did.
            ->...
            <<#EndSimple>>
        <<#EndIf>>
    <<#EndIf>>
    //if最後不信任司徒
    <<#If($DecideTrustSituOrNot=-1)>>
        Jason Dai: You don't trust her, either, yes?
    <<#EndIf>>
    Jason Dai: ...
    Jason Dai: I don't like her for what happened today.
    Jason Dai: The fact is she did help Fran and me. I won't deny it.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Jason Dai: ...
Jason Dai: These days I'm starting to see the true face of reality.
Jason Dai: The reality is that I never had any control over my own life.
Jason Dai: Genetically engineered from birth, hunted down by Vision, cooperating with Situ...
Jason Dai: I didn't have a choice in any of it.
Jason Dai: ...
Jason Dai: The most ironic part is...
Jason Dai: I don't even get to control my own body, right now.
Jason Dai: Because of you.
<<#Simple(8)>>
->I'm sorry, Jason.
    //
->I said it's not my choice.
    Jason Dai: I know, and I don't blame you.
    <<#Simple(6)>>
    ->Why bring it up, then?;<<Set($RelationshipWithMC,$-1)>>
        Jason Dai: I'm sorry, okay?
        Jason Dai: Let's stop this subject here.
        Jason Dai: Listen...
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Jason Dai: ...
Jason Dai: I know I'm not the kind who takes initiative in things...
Jason Dai: All I ever wanted is to be left alone, away from the world's and other people's problems.
Jason Dai: See nothing. Hear nothing. Do nothing.
Jason Dai: No matter how hard I try...
Jason Dai: They just won't leave me be.
Jason Dai: For every inch I give, they want two more.
Jason Dai: ...
Jason Dai: Now I've made up my mind.
Jason Dai: Fran has been right all along.
Jason Dai: I can't sit around and do nothing.
Jason Dai: Get my shit together, get a grip, take action.
Jason Dai: Don't just wait for things to happen.
<<#Simple(8)>>
->What's your plan?
    Jason Dai: I have no idea yet.
    Jason Dai: The bottom line is, now I know I must have an actionable plan.
->Fran started to see things your way. Doesn't that mean you were right?
    Jason Dai: No.
    Jason Dai: What happened is a wakeup call for both of us.
    Jason Dai: She's learned to avoid unnecessary risks.
    Jason Dai: And I've learned to take necessary risks.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Jason Dai: Although, I don't agree with Situ.
Jason Dai: She was talking about lofty ideas.
Jason Dai: In a sense, she's like Fran. They're both idealists.
Jason Dai: They want to change the world, serve the greater good. I don't care about it so much.
Jason Dai: As I said...
Jason Dai: Protect Fran. Protect the way of life we have. This is all I ask for.
Jason Dai: I want to take back what is rightfully mine.
Jason Dai: ...
Jason Dai: Starting from my body, of course.
<<#Simple(8)>>
->That's a good start.
    Jason Dai: Yeah.
->You have a plan, but it's not actionable.
    Jason Dai: ...Right.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
<<#End>>

title: 陳木海張延安麵店談話
body: <> Woody Chen: Hello, my kin. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Where's Ivan Zhang? Woody Chen: He will be here soon. ->Are we having the meeting here? Woody Chen: Yes. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Thought: He looks gloomy. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Is it really OK to talk about that in public? Woody Chen: Not a problem. Woody Chen: There won't be other customers at this hour. ->What do you expect from him? Woody Chen: Anything we could learn about the Source. Woody Chen: ... Woody Chen: Ideally, we will find its location. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Woody Chen: Before we start, allow me to remind you. Woody Chen: Ivan Zhang won't be very nice to us. <<#Simple(8)>> ->I see. // ->Figured. He was possessed by us, after all. Woody Chen: Yes. DO NOT let him know that you possessed him before. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Woody Chen: ...He's here. <> <<#Wait(2)>> //老張出現坐下 //if有進過第三日老張街景事件 <<#If($TalkToChanYenOnStreet=0)>> Ivan Zhang: Huh? I've seen you before... Ivan Zhang: Fuck me... Thought: He's staring at me as if I'm some kind of alien... Ivan Zhang: He's one of you, too? Woody Chen: Yes. Ivan Zhang: That's one hell of a surprise... <<#EndIf>> //if沒有進過第三日老張街景事件 <<#If($TalkToChanYenOnStreet=-1)>> Ivan Zhang: Hey, don't tell me he's...? Woody Chen: He is. Ivan Zhang: ...I see. <<#EndIf>> Woody Chen: Shall we start? Ivan Zhang: Wait... Ivan Zhang: ... Thought: He's staring hard at Woody Chen and I... Ivan Zhang: You're using possessed bodies? Woody Chen: You're correct, although it's not by choice. Ivan Zhang: ... Ivan Zhang: I know you want to shed these bodies and get back to wherever you belong. Ivan Zhang: But give me one reason why I should help things like you? Woody Chen: You're in urgent need of information. Woody Chen: And I promise to provide what you need. Ivan Zhang: In case you don't trust me, consider this my down payment. Woody Chen: ... Woody Chen: Yasmine Song. Ivan Zhang: Yasmine!? What the...!? <<#Simple(6)>> ->What are you guys talking about? Woody Chen: Mr. Zhang and I have a deal. He will tell me what happened at the Source. Woody Chen: In return, I will tell him about members of his team possessed by MBUs. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Ivan Zhang: Since when...? Woody Chen: I'm not sure. An informed guess, less than one week ago. Ivan Zhang: One week... Ivan Zhang: ... Ivan Zhang: How do you know? Woody Chen: This is not— Ivan Zhang: How can I trust you if I don't know how this "informed guess" is made? Woody Chen: This is not part of the deal, Mr. Zhang. Ivan Zhang: ... Ivan Zhang: I can't tell at all... Ivan Zhang: And she's my... Woody Chen: ... Woody Chen: Humans identify each other by visual and audio features. Woody Chen: Subtle changes of personality is a grey area. It's only natural that you failed to notice. Ivan Zhang: You don't see yourself as a human, huh. Woody Chen: ... Woody Chen: Unfortunately, I do not know whether your statement is correct. Woody Chen: But that is precisely what this meeting is about. Woody Chen: I need knowledge from you, and— Ivan Zhang: I must do something. Woody Chen: Mr. Zhang. Ivan Zhang: I can't let Yasmine... Woody Chen: I strongly advise against taking any action, Mr. Zhang. Ivan Zhang: Who are you to advise me? Woody Chen: For your own interest, do not let Yasmine Song suspect that you know. Ivan Zhang: Am I supposed to treat that…thing…as one of our own? Woody Chen: Yes. Woody Chen: That thing has complete control over your comrade. Woody Chen: It has a million ways to hurt the body before you can intervene. Ivan Zhang: ... Woody Chen: If she has recently been behaving in an acceptable way... Woody Chen: That means this kin of mine prefers to continue the life of her host. Woody Chen: Feigning ignorance is the most practical solution. Ivan Zhang: Hmph. Easy for you to say. Ivan Zhang: You're only trying to protect your "kin". Woody Chen: And in the process, protect your comrade. Ivan Zhang: ... Ivan Zhang: Good. Ivan Zhang: OK. You want a story, I give you a story. Ivan Zhang: But as I said, this story doesn't mean a thing to your purpose. Woody Chen: ... Woody Chen: Please do go on. Ivan Zhang: ... Ivan Zhang: Let's not start from the beginning. Waste of time. Ivan Zhang: Anyway, I somehow rubbed your master the wrong way. Ivan Zhang: Before I knew it, I was caught and thrown into a staged hospital room of sorts. Ivan Zhang: Then one of your kin possessed me... Ivan Zhang: It forced me to get information out of my man. Ivan Zhang: It even made me... Woody Chen: Made you? Ivan Zhang: I'd rather not tell you. Woody Chen: Mr. Zhang, please honor our deal— Ivan Zhang: I said I'm not telling!! Woody Chen: ... Woody Chen: I understand. Please continue. Ivan Zhang: ...After that, there was this creepy figure. Ivan Zhang: It killed everyone there. It didn't make damn sense. Woody Chen: Didn't make sense? Ivan Zhang: Like, he held two people up without touching them and crushed them mid-air. Woody Chen: This is... Ivan Zhang: Then I passed out. Woody Chen: ... Ivan Zhang: When I came around, I was in the same room. Ivan Zhang: I thought I must get the hell out of there, so I left the room... Ivan Zhang: And found myself in this dense fog. Can't see a thing, except for occasional strange machine high up. Ivan Zhang: The fog was probably laced with something. In just a few seconds, I started to feel dizzy. Ivan Zhang: So I plucked a ventilator from a dead body nearby and put it on. Ivan Zhang: That was when I figured I was not abducted by fucking aliens. Ivan Zhang: Those bodies, your masters, they're human enough. Woody Chen: ... Ivan Zhang: I tried to find a way out, or any living person. Nope. Blood everywhere. Ivan Zhang: I went on and on and on, nothing but more fog. Ivan Zhang: Just when I thought I was going to die there, I heard a low humming sound. Ivan Zhang: Following the sound, I found a gigantic steel gate... Ivan Zhang: The gate is connected to a heap of machines that I've never seen before. That was the source of the humming. Woody Chen: Could you tell what those machines are for? Did you see any label or instruction? Ivan Zhang: I'm not your genius scientist. Ivan Zhang: As to labels, there were some. I didn't pay much attention but... Woody Chen: But? Ivan Zhang: ...Summa Finalis Ivan Zhang: That was the only label I remember. Woody Chen: Summa Finalis? Ivan Zhang: No idea what the hell that means. Woody Chen: Please continue. Ivan Zhang: The gate opened as I approached. Ivan Zhang: Outside was an open field. I rushed out before it closed. Ivan Zhang: Then I found myself by a deserted service road, somewhere on Yangming Mountain. Ivan Zhang: Because I could see Power Station 4 far away. Ivan Zhang: I took a breath and turned back, and... Ivan Zhang: There's nothing. Woody Chen: There's nothing!? Ivan Zhang: Yep. One big open field. I was the tallest thing around. Woody Chen: Wait... Woody Chen: Where's the gate? You just went through a gate! Woody Chen: You should at least see the gate itself, if not the structure it's attached to! Woody Chen: Can you describe it? Ivan Zhang: You're not listening. Ivan Zhang: There's no structure. Woody Chen: No structure...? Woody Chen: That's abs— Ivan Zhang: See? The story doesn't make sense! Don't say I didn't warn you. Ivan Zhang: Kind of a fairytale. Woody Chen: Do you have the precise location? Ivan Zhang: I can send you the coordinates, but don't expect to find anything. Ivan Zhang: I went back to double check. Nothing. No gate, no structure. Woody Chen: ... Woody Chen: ...What did you do next? Ivan Zhang: What else? Ivan Zhang: Had to find my way back to human civilization. Ivan Zhang: I ran into a powerline service truck. A young guy was working on the cables. Ivan Zhang: He gave me a lift back to Wanpei. Woody Chen: ... Woody Chen: I...;//陳木海陷入沉思 Ivan Zhang: Now, question time. Ivan Zhang: Don't count on me for answers, though. I'm as clueless as you are. <> <> <> <<@問張延安逃脫經歷問題組>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Could this be related to Vision? ($DLStateFloat) <> Ivan Zhang: 200% positive it's not. Ivan Zhang: I've done business with Vision, countless times, mostly under the table. Ivan Zhang: I know pretty well what they can do. Ivan Zhang: That facility is beyond big corporations and their secret labs. Ivan Zhang: You heard the story. Do you believe Vision is capable that shit? <<#Simple(8)>> ->Who else could it be? ->Maybe Vision does have some secret tech. Ivan Zhang: I know people these days are used to the idea of secret tech. Ivan Zhang: Believe me, what I saw belongs to science fiction. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Ivan Zhang: I don't know if the term "common sense" means the same thing to whatever you are as to us humans. Ivan Zhang: Haven't you realized? Your own existence is against common sense. Ivan Zhang: Intangible minds that possess people? Is that realistic? <<#Simple(6)>> ->Good point. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Ivan Zhang: ... Ivan Zhang: Anyway, these people have technologies that are far beyond any corporation. Ivan Zhang: Ha! Ivan Zhang: Maybe they are aliens, after all. <<#GoTo(@問張延安逃脫經歷問題組)>> ->Do you trust your memory? ($DLStateFloat2) <> Ivan Zhang: Are you suggesting I bumped my head and dreamed this up? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I mean, could somebody have tampered with your memory? ->More or less. <<#EndSimple>> Ivan Zhang: How am I supposed to tell if someone did fuck with my mind? <<#Simple(8)>> ->Can't argue with that. // ->Didn't you mention inhaling some of that fog? Ivan Zhang: Right. No idea what's in it. Ivan Zhang: There's one thing I can guarantee. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Ivan Zhang: Every word I told you is true. Ivan Zhang: You can doubt the state of my mind, and that's all up to you. <<#GoTo(@問張延安逃脫經歷問題組)>> ->What did you do to Albert Tsai? ($DLStateFloat3) <> Ivan Zhang: ...!? Ivan Zhang: You... Ivan Zhang: You shouldn't know about Albert. I haven't told anyone... Ivan Zhang: Unless... One of you is the one who possessed me! Ivan Zhang: Was it you!? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Sorry, not me. Ivan Zhang: ... Ivan Zhang: Is that so? Ivan Zhang: Then tell whoever it is. //if就啊和 <<#If($WrongSyringe=true)>> Ivan Zhang: Tell it that I appreciate. <<#Simple(6)>> ->I thought you'd hate it? Ivan Zhang: No. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Ivan Zhang: It could have followed instructions, but chose not to. Ivan Zhang: Because of that, my man got to live. Ivan Zhang: I owe it one. <<#EndIf>> //if沒就啊和 <<#If($WrongSyringe=false)>> Ivan Zhang: Tell it that what it owes me, I will take back. <<#Simple(8)>> ->It was just following orders. Ivan Zhang: Yeah, he chose to follow orders. Ivan Zhang: There's a price for that choice. ->I will. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> Ivan Zhang: ... Ivan Zhang: As to Albert... Ivan Zhang: The whole process, I was carrying him. Ivan Zhang: Can't leave him there alone, can I? <<#GoTo(@問張延安逃脫經歷問題組)>> ->Yeah, it's me. <> Ivan Zhang: ... //if就阿翰 <<#If($WrongSyringe=true)>> Ivan Zhang: You chose to defy their order. Ivan Zhang: Albert would have been dead if not for you. Ivan Zhang: I owe you one. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Don't you hate me? I hurt Albert with your hands. Ivan Zhang: No. Ivan Zhang: You tried your best. I can tell. Ivan Zhang: Or at least, so I believe. ->You owe me nothing. Ivan Zhang: I'll be the judge of that. Ivan Zhang: And I decide that I owe you one. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Ivan Zhang: Keep that in mind. Ivan Zhang: ... Ivan Zhang: As to what I did to Albert... Ivan Zhang: The whole process, I was carrying him. Ivan Zhang: Can't leave him there alone, can I? <<#GoTo(@問張延安逃脫經歷問題組)>> <<#EndIf>> //if沒救阿翰 <<#If($WrongSyringe=false)>> Ivan Zhang: Is that so...? Ivan Zhang: Good. What you owe me, I will take it back. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Wait. I was forced. Ivan Zhang: I know you were following orders. Ivan Zhang: But you could have chosen not to. <<#Simple(10)>> ->You don't understand. I didn't have a choice. Ivan Zhang: So you say. ->I would have resisted if I could. Ivan Zhang: If you wanted. ->I know I'm liable, and for that I'm sorry. Ivan Zhang: ... Ivan Zhang: Your sorry won't bring Albert back. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Ivan Zhang: I won't do anything against you now. ->Take it back now, if you want. Ivan Zhang: Not yet. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Ivan Zhang: I keep my record straight. Whoever owes me must pay. Ivan Zhang: But Jason Dai is innocent. Ivan Zhang: I won't take any action until you're out of his body. Ivan Zhang: That much you can count on. <<#GoTo(@問張延安逃脫經歷問題直接結束)>> <<#EndIf>> <<#EndSimple>> ->I'm out of questions. Ivan Zhang: Yeah. <<#EndSimple>> <<@問張延安逃脫經歷問題直接結束>> <<#Wait(2)>> Ivan Zhang: Hey, don't you have anything to say? Woody Chen: ... Woody Chen: I... <<#Simple(6)>> ->What's up? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Woody Chen: I'm thinking about this "creepy figure" that obliterated everyone at the Source... Woody Chen: Who is it? Is it part of the Source? Ivan Zhang: ... Ivan Zhang: It was wearing the same protective suit as theirs... Ivan Zhang: But under the suit, it must be something else. Woody Chen: ... Woody Chen: What's the motive? Woody Chen: What does this figure want? Ivan Zhang: It has to be one of these cases. Ivan Zhang: One, to kill everyone in this "Source" of yours. Ivan Zhang: Two, to do something else. Those people happened to be in its way. Woody Chen: Something else, for example, to liberate all MBUs? Ivan Zhang: Beats me. Woody Chen: ... Woody Chen: The result is the same, though. Woody Chen: Your "Source" is no more. Woody Chen: Now we are free. Ivan Zhang: Free, in stolen bodies. Woody Chen: ... Woody Chen: For this reason, we must locate our own bodies asap. Woody Chen: That would be true freedom. Ivan Zhang: ... Ivan Zhang: In that sense, we have a shared goal. Ivan Zhang: To save Yasmine, I must solve your problem first. Ivan Zhang: Let's keep sharing what we manage to learn, if you don't have any objection. Woody Chen: I don't. Ivan Zhang: That's it for today? Ivan Zhang: We have exhausted our information. Woody Chen: Agreed. Woody Chen: Going forward, I will try to find out more about the Source. Woody Chen: You will receive updates when there is any. Woody Chen: This will take a while. Do not expect anything soon. Ivan Zhang: Good. Ivan Zhang: ...I need a cigarette. Ivan Zhang: Feel free to stay here before I close shop. //老張到外面抽菸 <> <<#Wait(3)>> Woody Chen: ... Woody Chen: Kin. Woody Chen: There is one thing I didn't tell him. Woody Chen: About the figure that destroyed the Source. Woody Chen: ... Woody Chen: You were possessing Ivan Zhang back then. You must have witnessed the process. Woody Chen: Do you understand what that means? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->No. // ->I can tell you more about the figure. Woody Chen: No. <<#EndSimple>> Woody Chen: It means you're transferred to Jason Dai after the Source was destroyed. Woody Chen: All our kin, myself included, were dispatched before that. Woody Chen: With the Source gone, we're trapped in our current bodies. Woody Chen: Do you see the problem? Woody Chen: Theoretically, no one should be able to send you into Jason Dai. Woody Chen: ... Woody Chen: Except for that mysterious figure, that is. Thought: ... Woody Chen: Now think again about what Ivan Zhang just said. Woody Chen: I have a feeling that you are its purpose. Woody Chen: It wiped out the Source to send you out and make sure no one comes after you. Woody Chen: The freedom to the rest of our kin is only a byproduct. <<#Simple(10)>> ->Do you really think so? Woody Chen: I don't know. ->So much trouble, just for me? // ->... <<#EndSimple>> Woody Chen: It's only a theory. Woody Chen: But I've got to say... Woody Chen: Right now, it looks scarily plausible. <<#Simple(10)>> ->What does that mean? ->What does it want? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Woody Chen: ... Woody Chen: Take the rest with a big pinch of salt. It's my imagination doing the work. Woody Chen: I think it wants to observe you. Thought: Observe...? Woody Chen: The evidence being it has taken no action or whatsoever yet. Woody Chen: No contact, no interference. Woody Chen: It even eliminated all possible interference for you—the Source. Woody Chen: It could be hiding somewhere, watching you. Woody Chen: ... Woody Chen: Of course, I could be wrong. Woody Chen: I just feel that maybe you could use this information. Woody Chen: As to how to act on it, all up to you. <<#Simple(10)>> ->I see. Woody Chen: Yeah. ->Thank you. Woody Chen: You don't need to thank me for anything. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#Wait(3)>> Woody Chen: ... Woody Chen: And I have noticed something... Woody Chen: Over time, we seem to be developing more initiative. Woody Chen: Once we were mindless puppets, but now we are starting to want things. Woody Chen: Everyone's want is unique, but there's something in common... Woody Chen: We all want to make a difference with this "stolen" time. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Make a difference? Woody Chen: To people around us, to the world in general... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Woody Chen: As if we're trying to prove something. Woody Chen: Prove our own existence. Woody Chen: If you're in the middle of something... Woody Chen: Do it with all your heart while you still can. Woody Chen: We don't know if this freedom will last forever. Thought: ... Woody Chen: Now, we should part ways. Woody Chen: Go back to our own lives. Forget about this. Woody Chen: Seize the day. Woody Chen: Farewell. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Bye. // ->Farewell, kin. Thought: He left me an understanding smile. ->... <<#EndSimple>> //玩家淡出到外面，陳木海回到陷入沉思 <<#End>>

Haha... Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: Okay, I've said it all.;<> Lawrence Chang: It's already bedtime. Lawrence Chang: See you tomorrow, Jason. <<#Simple(6)>> ->See you tomorrow. ->... <<#EndSimple>> //這裡攝影機移到主角身上 <> <<#Wait(2)>> Thought: ...;<> Thought: Why does he want to bring up the past, all of a sudden...? Thought: ... Thought: It's getting cold. Thought: Better get toing. //if麵店 <<#If($ToldWoodSeaChangStory=2)>> Thought: I have a meeting appointment. <<#EndIf>> <> <<#End>> <<#EndSimple>> <<@隆生說悲慘過去故事時間>> Lawrence Chang: ... Thought: Lawrence's face darkens... Lawrence Chang: I skipped a lot of details last time...;<> Lawrence Chang: We, I and my family, were not the only victim of the accident at Tendril. Lawrence Chang: There were about forty victims. Lawrence Chang: The accident made it to the press, but was covered up soon enough. Lawrence Chang: Back then, flop farming was a superstar technology. Everyone was talking about its positive side. Lawrence Chang: Compared to that, killing a few dozen Shithole residents is hardly a cost. Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: There's something people didn't know about that accident... Lawrence Chang: Actually, no one died when it happened.;<> Thought: But he said his family all died in the accident, didn't he? <<#Simple(8)>> ->Isn't that a good thing? Lawrence Chang: ...Yeah. Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: Except for what happened afterward. ->But your family? Lawrence Chang: They were not dead, not by then. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: Comatose. The accident turned us into vegetables.;<> Lawrence Chang: Tendril's first response was to salvage what they could. Lawrence Chang: We were transferred to one of their secret labs. Lawrence Chang: There they tried many procedures to cure us. Nothing worked. A few died in the process.;<> Lawrence Chang: In the end, they gave up. Lawrence Chang: But it was too late. They couldn't just claim nothing happened. Lawrence Chang: So they decided to make a public announcement, saying we were all killed in the accident. Lawrence Chang: One thing leads to another... Lawrence Chang: Now they have a bunch of vegetable subjects. What should they do with us...?;<> <<#Simple(6)>> ->What did they do? // ->Subjects? Lawrence Chang: Test subjects. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: For months, their lab used us for experiments. Lawrence Chang: Starting with innocuous ones, testing body response, so on and so forth... Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: Then they started radical procedures. Cutting off organs and limbs, taking brains apart...;<> Lawrence Chang: I was conscious the whole time. I could see what they did to me... Lawrence Chang: We were kept in a dark room, fed through tubes. Lawrence Chang: They push us out to be operated on.;<> Lawrence Chang: Some of us never came back. Lawrence Chang: Every single day, I prayed in silence. Not me, please not me, don't pick me...;<> Lawrence Chang: I even... Lawrence Chang: ...;<> Thought: He retched. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Stop. That's quite enough. ->You what? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: I even asked whatever god there is to make them take my parents... Lawrence Chang: Anyone! Anyone but me.;<> <<#Simple(6)>> ->Don't blame yourself. You were under crushing fear. This is normal. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: I tried to count the days.;<> Lawrence Chang: But it was next to impossible in a windowless room. Lab staff visits were my only clue. Lawrence Chang: Soon I gave up on that... Lawrence Chang: I thought that would be the end of me, of us all.;<> Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: One day, after an experiment, I found myself able to move and talk again...;<> Lawrence Chang: I begged them to let me and my family go. Lawrence Chang: Of course they wouldn't let us. I was drugged and put back to the bed. Lawrence Chang: Before I passed out, though, I noticed something different about the lab staff.;<> Lawrence Chang: On their faces was something that wasn't there before... Lawrence Chang: It was surprise, shock, also... Lawrence Chang: Sympathy? Lawrence Chang: As if they didn't realize I'm a human until I started to talk.;<> Lawrence Chang: Before that, I was a lab rat. Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: That changed things. Lawrence Chang: A few hours later, one guy came and released me.;<> Lawrence Chang: He wanted to let me go, against lab rule. But I cried, asked him to take my parents with us. Lawrence Chang: No surprise. We couldn't carry two adults with us. Lawrence Chang: In the process, more lab staff heard us. We had to flee.;<> Lawrence Chang: I managed to escape the facility, but the man who saved me was caught. Lawrence Chang: I left him behind, left my parents behind.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->You didn't mean to, Lawrence. Lawrence Chang: That does not change the fact. Lawrence Chang: ... ->You couldn't have done anything even if you stayed. Lawrence Chang: I knew. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: I knew I couldn't do anything back then. Lawrence Chang: But I swore to come back one day, and save my parents. Lawrence Chang: ...;<> Lawrence Chang: Out of the facility, I found a way to join a gang. They were nice enough to take me in. Lawrence Chang: I stole some guns from them, and went back to the Tendril facility. <<#Simple(8)>> ->On your own? Lawrence Chang: Yeah, I knew I had no chance.;<> <<@我那時滿腦子只有救出爸媽的想法>> Lawrence Chang: I must do something to help my parents, before it's too late. Lawrence Chang: Every single day counts...;<> ->How old were you again? Lawrence Chang: Ha, I don't remember...;<> Lawrence Chang: How old is old enough to raid a research facility, guns blazing? Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: I knew it was a trip of no return. <<#GoTo(@我那時滿腦子只有救出爸媽的想法)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: Just when I was getting close to the facility... Lawrence Chang: I ran into a woman with silver hair.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Situ. Lawrence Chang: Yeah. ->A granny out for hiking. Lawrence Chang: Haha, no. Lawrence Chang: It's Nin. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: She stopped my suicidal mission.;<> Lawrence Chang: Just when I was arguing with her... Lawrence Chang: Boom! The facility went up in flames. Lawrence Chang: Later she told me that she went inside. Whoever it was that I tried to save, they were all dead.;<> Lawrence Chang: She blew the place up so that the press would notice. Lawrence Chang: Her plan partially worked. Tendril fired a row of executives, and sent a few scapegoats to the police.;<> Lawrence Chang: To me, it was better than nothing. Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: After that, I lost track of Nin. She just vanished.;<> Lawrence Chang: Without leaving behind even a name. Lawrence Chang: Her existence told me there were people actually busy trying to save the world. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Why didn't she tell you her name? Lawrence Chang: She didn't have a reason to. I was just another brat. Haha... ->How did you find her again? Lawrence Chang: That's a story for another time.;<> Lawrence Chang: Simply put, I've been looking for her ever since. Lawrence Chang: There were a few cases where she needed helpers, I enlisted myself. <> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: ... <<#Wait(2)>> Lawrence Chang: ...;<> Lawrence Chang: With this story, Jason, I'm trying to make you understand what Nin and flop farming mean to me. Thought: He is very determined now. Lawrence Chang: They are two forces that drive me forward.;<> Lawrence Chang: The pursuit of justice, of power. Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: I am a victim of the flop farming industry.;<> //if有說恨運算產業 <<#If($AskRonHateComputBusiness=0)>> Lawrence Chang: Now, if you ask whether or not I hate this industry... Lawrence Chang: I'm not ashamed to say I hate it with all my heart. <<#EndIf>> Lawrence Chang: But after everything that happened, I realized... Lawrence Chang: What I hate the most is the fact that some people walk away untouched after committing unspeakable evil.;<> Lawrence Chang: I take it personally. This is outrageous. Lawrence Chang: It's UNFAIR. Lawrence Chang: I must do something to settle the score. I must make someone pay. <<#Simple(8)>> ->I know the feeling. Thought: No, I don't care about what happens to other people. Lawrence Chang: I'm so glad that you understand. ->This is exactly why Situ is worried about you. Lawrence Chang: ...;<> Lawrence Chang: Yeah, she said something like that, too. Lawrence Chang: But I am who I am. <> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: Having survived it, I feel the rage, the fear. Lawrence Chang: Whoever caused it must pay.;<> Lawrence Chang: This is what I call justice, what I pursue with Nin and 23. Thought: Using justice as a driving force, huh?;<> Thought: Can't relate at all. Lawrence Chang: Oh.;<> Lawrence Chang: I told you I want to join OWL, didn't I?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yeah, you did that. // ->Did you? Lawrence Chang: I did when we were in the elevator. <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: I'm dead serious about it. I want to be like Nin. <<#Simple(8)>> ->You said something about the power to do the right thing. Lawrence Chang: Ugh. It feels pretentious, hearing it from you...;<> Thought: He actually blushed...? Lawrence Chang: B-but that is right.;<> ->So? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: These days, it takes power to wrestle against those with power. Lawrence Chang: Nin is a living example. Lawrence Chang: Remember what 23 said about Nin beating the adversity test?;<> Lawrence Chang: That is the power I'm talking about. Lawrence Chang: I've seen how Nin handles dilemmas, multiple times.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->For example? Lawrence Chang: For example, once OWL told them to handle terrorists who were about to disclose classified information to the public.;<> Lawrence Chang: OWL's instruction was to blow up the building they were in, sacrificing all civilians. Lawrence Chang: Nin found a way to cut out the building's network connection for about five minutes. Lawrence Chang: Within those five minutes, she rushed in and neutralized all terrorists, over 30, one by one.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->On her own? Lawrence Chang: Yep.;<> Thought: Because of her genetic engineering...? Thought: Does it mean I can do it, too? ->Is that one of OWL's adversity tests? Lawrence Chang: Eh? Now that I think about it, most likely yes. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: My point is... ->I can imagine. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: I've never seen her hesitating, or wavering. She analyses the situation and takes actions.;<> Lawrence Chang: I want to be like her. Lawrence Chang: With that kind of ability and willpower, I will be able to do anything.;<> //if有追問司徒 <<#If($NinExplainWhyWorryRon=0)>> Thought: This is what Situ complained about... Thought: Unrealistic optimism. <<#EndIf>> Lawrence Chang: I know I'm far from that goal, but I'm working on it! <<#Simple(8)>> ->Effort doesn't necessarily translate into result. Lawrence Chang: Hey! Don't rain on my parade with that old geezer talk. <<#Simple(8)>> ->I mean it. ->Sorry. Thought you needed a reality check. Lawrence Chang: ...I know. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->Keep working toward it, Lawrence. Lawrence Chang: I will!;<> Lawrence Chang: Thank you, Jason! ->Situ is only human. Don't idolize her too much. Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: YESSIR!;<> Thought: Ah, my ears... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: ...;<> Lawrence Chang: You must be wondering? <<#Simple(6)>> ->About what? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: That I'm boring you out with this looong story?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Not at all. Thank you for sharing. Lawrence Chang: Ha! You're a great listener! Lawrence Chang: There's a purpose in telling you my story. ->May I know why? <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: Because we're buddies, Jason!;<> Lawrence Chang: You should know more about me! Lawrence Chang: And I'd like to know more about you, too. Find me any time when you wanna share something. Thought: ...;<> <<#Simple(6)>> ->Thank you, Lawrence. Lawrence Chang: Eh? You don't need to. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: Let's be really good friends going forward! Thought: Going forward... Thought: Huh...;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: YEE-HAW!!!!;<>;<> Thought: ...That's a bit too much drama. Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: HA! Finally, I can talk about it with someone! Lawrence Chang: I've been waiting for this day like forever. Lawrence Chang: Couldn't talk to Nin or 23 about it.;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: They like me well enough, but I just couldn't see them as simple friends. Lawrence Chang: I know you wouldn't believe it, but I do feel lonely sometimes.;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: Luckily! Now I have you! Lawrence Chang: You're like an older brother, you know?;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Eeeewww. Lawrence Chang: Hey! Gimme some brotherly love! ->Haha. Lawrence Chang: I can't tell if you mean it or not. Lawrence Chang: I'd just presume you're happy to hear it! ->I appreciate your friendship, too. Lawrence Chang: Haha! What's that diplomatic talk for! ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: ...;<> Lawrence Chang: Okay, it's getting late... Lawrence Chang: About time for me to hit the hay.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Yeah. // ->Yay, finally! Lawrence Chang: What, am I that boring? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Haha! // ->I took, like, two quick naps. Lawrence Chang: Hey! ->Better than Situ's moral lectures, I'll give you that. Lawrence Chang: Hahahahaha! Lawrence Chang: I'll tell her you said that. <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: See you tomorrow, Jason!;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->See you tomorrow. // ->Get some rest. Lawrence Chang: You bet! ->... <<#EndSimple>> //這裡攝影機移到主角身上 <> <<#Wait(2)>> Thought: Everyone has a dark past, even an optimistic boy like him...;<> Thought: ... Thought: Human test subjects... Thought: Fran nearly getting killed... Thought: Bad shit's been happening left and right all the time. Thought: I just didn't see them until they hit me right in the face...;<> Thought: The world is moving on, ever so slowly, without me knowing. Thought: ... Thought: But my priority remains unchanged. Thought: Defend my way of life and the people I care about.;<> Thought: That's it. Thought: ... Thought: It's getting cold. Thought: Better get going. //if麵店 <<#If($ToldWoodSeaChangStory=2)>> Thought: I have a meeting appointment. <<#EndIf>> <> <<#End>>
