title: 司徒跟主角程芳語重心長2
body: Frances Cheng: JASON!;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->What the hell are you doing? ->I'm OK, Fran. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: Sorry.;<>;//司徒寧把黑樁收回去，主角轉身重新面朝司徒寧 Nin Situ: I suppose a live demonstration works better than a lecture.;<> Nin Situ: As you can see, Jason is not your average human. Frances Cheng: ...Not average human?;<> Nin Situ: He just demonstrated superhuman reflex.;<> Frances Cheng: You lost me...;<>;<>;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Does it have to do with genetic engineering? ->You knew what I am. What did you do that for? Frances Cheng: J? You know what Ms. Situ is talking about? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: I'll get straight to the point, Frances.;<> Nin Situ: Jason was genetically engineered since he was born. Frances Cheng: Engineered? By whom?;<> Thought: Fran is freaking out. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Calm down, Fran. Frances Cheng: How am I supposed to calm down? ->It's not the harmful kind of engineering. Frances Cheng: R-really? Nin Situ: Yes. Thought: She seems calming a little. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: Please allow me to explain.;<>;<> Nin Situ: OWL found out that an unidentified secret society has been genetically engineering infants for decades.;<> Nin Situ: These infants are susceptible to remote control. They can be used as puppets. Frances Cheng: Remote control?;<> Nin Situ: According to our dossier, engineered humans have one thing in common—;<> Nin Situ: Occasional out-of-character behaviors, occurring in sporadic episodes. Nin Situ: This is the result of remote control by the secret society. That much we have confirmed.;<> Frances Cheng: ...;<> Nin Situ: Each control session could last from ten minutes to three or four days. Frances Cheng: You mean Jason is...!;<> Nin Situ: He is now shielded. Remote control won't work on him anymore. Frances Cheng: ...;<> Frances Cheng: This is too much to process...;<> Frances Cheng: I need some time. Nin Situ: I understand.;<> Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: When did you find out, J?;<>;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Last night. // ->Not much sooner than you did. Frances Cheng: When? Thought: She is demanding a definite answer. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Last night. // ->Um, Situ? When was that again? Thought: The way she frowns is effectively saying "Really?" Nin Situ: ...Last night. <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: Why didn't you tell me then? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I didn't want to make your world any more complicated than it already was. Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: You're always like that. ->I haven't found a proper chance yet. Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: Right. Too many things are happening at the same time... <<#EndSimple>> Thought: I can't tell if she's convinced. Frances Cheng: Ms. Situ...;<>;<> Frances Cheng: Why tell me this? Why now? Nin Situ: Because I just made sure of one thing.;<> Nin Situ: What I didn't tell you is that there are many types of engineering. Nin Situ: There is one particularly dangerous kind, with elevated physical capabilities.;<>;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->What kind of capabilities? Nin Situ: Simply put, faster, stronger. ->I'm one of that kind? Nin Situ: Yes. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: They're effectively weaponized humans.;<> Nin Situ: You, Jason, are a humanoid weapon. <<#Simple(8)>> ->What does that indicate? Nin Situ: You're a more significant threat than other engineered subjects. ->That's why I managed to beat those mercenaries? Nin Situ: I believe so. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: But if you already made him immune to remote control, then...?;<> Nin Situ: ...;<> Nin Situ: OWL has special rules regarding humanoid weapons... Nin Situ: Contain indefinitely in a research facility, or exterminate on sight. Frances Cheng: What!?;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->You seriously want to do that? ->I won't go down without a fight. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: Calm down. I won't allow that to happen.;<> Frances Cheng: But you can't change OWL's rules...;<> Nin Situ: Yes.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->What if you don't tell them about me? Nin Situ: They will find out. Matter of time. Nin Situ: I can cover up for you, although it won't change the result. ->So, you were just announcing my death sentence? Nin Situ: No. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: There is a third way. Nin Situ: Join OWL, like I did.;<> Frances Cheng: ...;<>;<> Nin Situ: I'm a humanoid weapon myself.;<> Thought: She is one, too?;<> Nin Situ: More than a few leaders of OWL want to make good use of people like us.;<> Nin Situ: I am one of the few poster children.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->The few? ->Not everyone made it? Nin Situ: Those considered particularly unstable, or not fit for field operations... Nin Situ: They had to be either contained or exterminated. Nin Situ: What I mean is... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: If you trust me enough, I hope to solve your problem with this approach. Thought: Joining OWL...?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I need to think about it. ->I don't have any other choice. Nin Situ: Yeah. ->I can go into hermit mode. Nin Situ: If you don't mind being hunted down for the rest of your life. <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: What would you suggest, Frances?;<> Frances Cheng: ...;<> Frances Cheng: I don't know. Frances Cheng: I can't make big decisions like this on the spot.;<> Nin Situ: You're right. Sorry. Nin Situ: ...;<>;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Am I really the weaponized type? Nin Situ: We need lab tests for an official verdict, but I'm 100% sure. <<#GoTo(@妳為什麼不一開始就跟我說這些？啞啞)>> ->Why didn't you tell me before? <<@妳為什麼不一開始就跟我說這些？啞啞>> Nin Situ: I need time to evaluate you. Nin Situ: I checked your background before making contact.;<> Nin Situ: The way you subdued the stabber at the farm was self-evident enough.;<> Nin Situ: Your muscle power and reflex were obviously beyond what's normally considered possible. Nin Situ: At the hospital, I told Lawrence to watch how you handled those thugs.;<>;<> Nin Situ: Quick and clean, as expected. Nin Situ: On top of that, you dealt with professional mercenaries earlier today. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Okay. // ->But I'm just an average guy when not possessed. Frances Cheng: Is that so?;<> Nin Situ: You're supposed to blend in when not possessed.;<> Nin Situ: There are exceptional cases, though. You are one of such cases, Jason.;<> Nin Situ: And that is why I believe you will do well as an OWL agent.;<> Nin Situ: With training, you will be able to tap into your powers with your unpossessed mind, like me. Thought: Can I really do that...? ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: Jason, this may sound like a crisis, but it's also an opportunity. Nin Situ: Before you face OWL, I can help you to prepare.;<> <<#Simple(10)>> ->I know, and I won't let this opportunity slip. Nin Situ: Good.;<> Nin Situ: You have a strong mind. ->Great. Better than extermination. Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: Correct.;<> ->Sorry to disappoint, if you're expecting a thank you. Nin Situ: I didn't mean that... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: I know you need time to process. You both.;<> Nin Situ: Rest assured that OWL won't notice Jason in the immediate future.;<> Thought: How much time do we have?;<>;<> Thought: They will notice me sooner or later. Thought: When that happens, Fran... Frances Cheng: May I ask a question?;<>;<> Nin Situ: Ask away. Frances Cheng: This...these investigations... Frances Cheng: Some of them against the order of your superior... Frances Cheng: And now, offering to help Jason.;<> Frances Cheng: ...Why are you doing this? Nin Situ: I always try to do the right thing.;<> Thought: She said that without thinking.;<> Thought: Lawrence said something like that, too...;<> Nin Situ: I do it because I can.;<>;<> Nin Situ: With great power comes great responsibility.;<> <<#Simple(10)>> ->Can't believe someone's actually putting that into practice. Nin Situ: Maybe not fully into practice, but I'm trying. ->Iron Man. Frances Cheng: No. Spider-Man.;<> Nin Situ: Correct. Thought: They shared a knowing smile.;<> ->Spider-Man. Nin Situ: Correct. Thought: She flashed a knowing smile. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: How far will you go...;<> Frances Cheng: To do what you think is the right thing? Nin Situ: As far as I need to, without crossing red lines. Nin Situ: Some measures are against my purposes. I won't shoot myself in the foot. Frances Cheng: You think you have strong principles. Nin Situ: Exactly the opposite. Nin Situ: I see principles as rigid shackles. More trouble than they're worth. Nin Situ: Weak principles are too loose to be of any value. Nin Situ: Strong, rigid principles only hold you down.;<> Nin Situ: Never kill, never lie, be loyal to someone, so on and so forth... Nin Situ: These principles slow you down when you really need to take action. Nin Situ: Worse still, you don't achieve anything extra by adhering to them.;<> Nin Situ: No help with action, no help with results. <<#Simple(8)>> ->But without principles, aren't you acting on a whim? Nin Situ: Everyone acts on a whim. Whatever we do, we make decisions by going with what we think is right at the time.;<> Nin Situ: Sometimes we consider consequences further down the road. Nin Situ: But on a grander scale, that's just acting on a slower whim. Nin Situ: My point is, as long as your deed serves your purpose, your pursuit...;<> Nin Situ: There's nothing wrong with acting on a whim. Frances Cheng: Many choose the path you described, but refuse to face the consequence.;<> Nin Situ: Indeed.;<> Nin Situ: More often than not, principles become excuses to indulge oneself. Nin Situ: "I'm adhering to these principles, so it must be OK if I do that." Nin Situ: They allow themselves the maximum indulgence by re-interpreting principles as they need.;<> ->What of adhering to principles is my very pursuit? Nin Situ: Respectable, but I'd call it stupid.;<> Nin Situ: It is possible to achieve something by holding tight onto principles, although it doesn't happen often.;<> Nin Situ: You can earn, for example, people's trust. Nin Situ: Although there are much better ways to earn trust.;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->It's a showcase of willpower. Nin Situ: Then show your willpower with something that makes more practical sense.;<> Nin Situ: Holding onto principles doesn't mean much. Nin Situ: Saying "holding onto principles is my pursuit" is problematic in and of itself.;<> Nin Situ: Most of the time they have no idea what their pursuit is. ->I see. <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: I know myself fairly well. Nin Situ: Every choice I make is a weighted, informed decision.;<> Nin Situ: Instead of creating supposedly universal principles, I judge every situation as a separate case. Nin Situ: My decisions are made based on the value I assigned to everything. Nin Situ: Value is something I can talk about. It's an effective measurement of my own actions and their results.;<> Nin Situ: Value fluctuates all the time, though. Nin Situ: Worshipping rules or principles equals giving up independent thinking, which marks the start of a downward spiral. Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: Does 23 agree with you?;<> Nin Situ: I don't think so. Nin Situ: She has her own principles. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Respecting laws? ->Following OWL's orders? Nin Situ: In a loose sense, yes. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: But she broke her own principles by joining you on this unsanctioned operation. Nin Situ: ...Correct. Nin Situ: For this very reason...;<> Nin Situ: She's been struggling against herself since the start of this operation. Nin Situ: The world is a complicated place. No rigid principles could grant permanent peace of mind. Nin Situ: I tried to explain to her, but she wouldn't listen. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Peace of mind? <<@我們的工作經常讓我們面臨艱難的選擇。吧吧>> Nin Situ: This line of work often pits us against tough decisions.;<> Nin Situ: Sacrificing civilians for the sake of an operation is just one example.;<> Nin Situ: Many have trouble dealing with such pressure and guilt. 23 is one of them. Nin Situ: "I'm doing this for law and order." That gives her peace of mind.;<> Nin Situ: Now you see why she values principles.;<> Thought: But she broke her own principles... <<#Simple(8)>> ->Why is she breaking her own principles, do you think? Nin Situ: These principles are no longer providing her peace of mind.;<> Nin Situ: She chose to go after Vision, because it's obviously the right thing to do. Nin Situ: Yet her principles are giving her hell for doing the right thing...;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->What if she's doing it only for your sake? Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: In that case, my interpretation stands. Nin Situ: ...;<> Nin Situ: However... Nin Situ: It makes me sad.;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->You don't approve of her very much. Nin Situ: I don't approve of certain parts of her.;<>;<> Nin Situ: I care about her, a lot. She has my general approval. Nin Situ: ...;<>;<> <<#GoTo(@我們的工作經常讓我們面臨艱難的選擇。吧吧)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: I think I understand. Thank you.;<> Nin Situ: I'm glad you asked. Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: We are the same kind of people, Frances. Nin Situ: Instead of peace of mind, we choose to assess things in terms of right and wrong. Nin Situ: More importantly, we are both willing to make sacrifices to do the right thing. Frances Cheng: ...;<> Frances Cheng: Before today, I'd take that as a compliment. Frances Cheng: Now I'm not so sure.;<> Frances Cheng: I'm not willing to sacrifice. I just never saw it as a real possibility. Frances Cheng: ...;<> Frances Cheng: ...I was impulsive, not brave.;<> Nin Situ: You need time and experience to figure out how much leverage you can afford. Frances Cheng: ...Leverage?;<>;<> Frances Cheng: I'm not very comfortable with that word... Nin Situ: S-sorry, don't get me wrong... Thought: Huh, this is the first time I see Situ stutter.;<> Nin Situ: I just... Nin Situ: It was a poor choice of words. I apologize.;<> Nin Situ: Sometimes I overlook the sentimental value of people and things. Frances Cheng: No offense taken. I understand. Thought: Fran showed her a knowing smile. Nin Situ: What I meant to say is, we learn a little more about ourselves with each tough choice.;<> Frances Cheng: How tough is tough? Nin Situ: Having gone through so much, I must have dealt with a fair share of tough moments, no matter by what definition.;<> Frances Cheng: Of course... Frances Cheng: ...;<> Frances Cheng: But... Frances Cheng: Maybe you haven't seen what your level of "tough" is yet?;<> Thought: Her grin says "nice try". Nin Situ: You sound awfully like someone. Frances Cheng: I hope it's a good person. Nin Situ: A meticulous person. Frances Cheng: ... Nin Situ: ... //主角發出肚子餓的聲音 <>;<>;<> Frances Cheng: Haha, Jason is hungry. Thought: Man, philosophical debate burns through food fast. Nin Situ: Go grab some dinner. We have already covered all key points.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->What about you? Nin Situ: I'm not hungry. Frances Cheng: Me, neither. ->I'll find some grub downstairs then. Frances Cheng: Sure! ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: You might catch up with Lawrence and 23 if you're fast enough. Nin Situ: I will examine Frances's wound. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Let me help. Frances Cheng: You really don't have to! Nin Situ: I'd advise against it. Nin Situ: Unless you're trained in first aid. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Okay. // ->You could be more diplomatic, you know? ->Yeah yeah yeah, I'm useless, I get that. Nin Situ: I didn't mean—;<>;<> Frances Cheng: Haha, he is joking. Nin Situ: Ha, I see...;<>;<> <<#EndSimple>> ->Okay. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: See you later. Don't be too late! <<#Simple(6)>> ->See you later. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#End>>
